title: Aesthetics, Axes, and Secondary Scales
text: toy dataset pairs two incompatible measurements genomic positions: z-scored signal centered around zero, read-depth count 50–200 range. Plotting single y-scale crush one , natural fit secondary axis. %+% operator returns seq_plot invisibly, render plot assign expression p reference last line chunk — knitr auto-prints result via print.SeqPlot(), calls $plot() current device.
code: win <- GRanges("chr1", IRanges(1, 1e6)) d <- GRanges("chr1",              IRanges(seq(2e4, 1e6, by = 2e4), width = 1),              score = rnorm(50),              depth = round(rpois(50, 100) +                            40 * sin(seq(0, pi, length.out = 50))))

title: Aesthetics, Axes, and Secondary Scales
text: axis namespace four levels specificity: SeqPlotR resolves key, walks specific general: axis.x1.line.col -> axis.x.line.col -> axis.line.col -> built-default. first match wins.
code: p <- seq_plot() %+%   seq_track(data = d, windows = win,             aesthetics = aes(               axis.line.col    = "grey50",   # all axes               axis.y.line.lwd  = 1.5,        # both y axes               axis.x1.line.col = "firebrick" # primary x overrides             )) %+%   seq_point(map(x = start, y = score),             aesthetics = aes(color = "steelblue")) p

title: Aesthetics, Axes, and Secondary Scales
text: keys can written nested form. aes(axis.x = aes(position = "top")) identical aes(axis.x.position = "top"), components can grouped topic rather spelled one one.
code: p <- seq_plot() %+%   seq_track(data = d, windows = win,             aesthetics = aes(               axis.y = aes(                 line = aes(col = "grey20", lwd = 1.2),                 text = aes(size = 0.7)               )             )) %+%   seq_point(map(x = start, y = score)) p

title: Aesthetics, Axes, and Secondary Scales
text: axis’s scale options live axis.<side>.scale.*: scale.expand = c(mul, add) — proportional (mul times data range) absolute padding around data. Default c(0.025, 0). scale.breaks — explicit break positions. Otherwise n_breaks picks pretty() breaks. scale.minor_breaks — scalar (number sub-divisions) explicit vector. "capped" (default) runs first last break. "full" spans expanded plot range. "exact" spans unexpanded data range. "ticks" suppresses axis line shows tick marks.  Equivalent prefer explicit scale object:
code: p <- seq_plot() %+%   seq_track(data = d, windows = win,             aesthetics = aes(               axis.y1.scale.limits        = c(-3, 3),               axis.y1.scale.breaks        = c(-2, 0, 2),               axis.y1.scale.cap           = "capped",               axis.y1.scale.minor_breaks  = 3             )) %+%   seq_point(map(x = start, y = score)) p seq_plot() %+%   seq_track(data = d, windows = win,             scale_y = seq_scale_continuous(               limits       = c(-3, 3),               breaks       = c(-2, 0, 2),               minor_breaks = 3,               cap          = "capped"             )) %+%   seq_point(map(x = start, y = score))

title: Aesthetics, Axes, and Secondary Scales
text: element opts secondary axis adding axis.x = 2 axis.y = 2 map(). SeqPlotR infers independent scale elements, draws second axis, positions element new scale. default positions x1 = bottom, x2 = top, y1 = left, y2 = right.  Moving axis unusual position just matter setting position. axes side stack automatically, lower-indexed axis (x1, y1) sitting closer panel.
code: p <- seq_plot() %+%   seq_track(data = d, windows = win,             aesthetics = aes(               axis.y1.line.col = "firebrick",               axis.y2.line.col = "steelblue"             )) %+%   seq_point(map(x = start, y = score),             aesthetics = aes(color = "firebrick")) %+%   seq_line(map(x = start, y = depth, axis.y = 2),            aesthetics = aes(color = "steelblue", linewidth = 1.2)) p #> 50 out-of-bounds data points excluded! (seq_line) p <- seq_plot() %+%   seq_track(data = d, windows = win,             aesthetics = aes(               axis.x1.position = "top",               axis.x2.position = "top",               axis.x1.line.col = "firebrick",               axis.x2.line.col = "steelblue"             ),             scale_x2 = seq_scale_continuous(limits = c(0, 1))) %+%   seq_point(map(x = start, y = score)) p

title: Aesthetics, Axes, and Secondary Scales
text: track.* namespace controls rectangle shared windows; track.window.* controls per-window panel rectangle. inherit — ’re semantically distinct.
code: p <- seq_plot() %+%   seq_track(data = d, windows = win,             aesthetics = aes(               track.background.fill        = "#faf8f5",               track.border.col             = "grey30",               track.window.background.fill = "white",               track.window.border.col      = "grey60"             )) %+%   seq_point(map(x = start, y = score)) p

title: Aesthetics, Axes, and Secondary Scales
text: Gridlines drawn positions axis ticks, default. Enable per dimension axis.x.gridline = TRUE axis.y.gridline = TRUE aes() either seq_plot() seq_track(). follow dotted-path hierarchy every axis key — axis.gridline.* sets base defaults, axis.x.gridline.* / axis.y.gridline.* override per dimension.  can also pass aes() directly value. Supplying styling sub-keys enough enable gridline; separate = TRUE needed:  axes can shown together. shared base style comes axis.gridline.*, per-dimension keys override :  Gridlines respect standard plot-level vs track-level theme precedence. plot-level axis.y.gridline = TRUE suppressed track B via track-level override:
code: p <- seq_plot() %+%   seq_track(data = d, windows = win,             aesthetics = aes(               axis.y.gridline       = TRUE,               axis.y.gridline.color = "grey80",               axis.y.gridline.lwd   = 0.5             )) %+%   seq_point(map(x = start, y = score)) p p <- seq_plot() %+%   seq_track(data = d, windows = win,             aesthetics = aes(               axis.y.gridline = aes(color = "grey80", lwd = 0.5)             )) %+%   seq_point(map(x = start, y = score)) p p <- seq_plot() %+%   seq_track(data = d, windows = win,             aesthetics = aes(               axis.y.gridline       = TRUE,               axis.x.gridline       = TRUE,               axis.gridline.color   = "grey85",               axis.gridline.lwd     = 0.4,               axis.x.gridline.lty   = 2   # dashed x only             )) %+%   seq_point(map(x = start, y = score)) p win2 <- GRanges("chr1", IRanges(c(1, 1e6 + 1), c(1e6, 2e6)))  p <- seq_plot(aesthetics = aes(axis.y.gridline       = TRUE,                                axis.gridline.color   = "grey85")) %+%   seq_track(track_id = "A", data = d, windows = win2) %+%   seq_point(map(x = start, y = score)) %__%   seq_track(track_id = "B", data = d, windows = win2,             aesthetics = aes(axis.y.gridline = FALSE)) %+%   seq_line(map(x = start, y = depth), aesthetics = aes(color = "steelblue")) p

title: Aesthetics, Axes, and Secondary Scales
text: Themes set seq_plot() apply every track; themes seq_track() override track . precedence ggplot2’s theme() plus per-facet overrides.
code: win2 <- GRanges("chr1", IRanges(c(1, 1e6 + 1), c(1e6, 2e6)))  p <- seq_plot(aesthetics = aes(     axis.line.col    = "grey30",     axis.text.size   = 0.55,     track.border.col = "grey70"   )) %+%   seq_track(track_id = "A", data = d, windows = win2) %+%   seq_point(map(x = start, y = score)) %__%   seq_track(track_id = "B", data = d, windows = win2,             aesthetics = aes(axis.y1.line.col = "firebrick")) %+%   seq_line(map(x = start, y = depth), aesthetics = aes(color = "firebrick")) p

title: Aesthetics, Axes, and Secondary Scales
text: axis component: axis[.side].{line,ticks,text,title}.{col,lwd,size,angle,visible}. scale option: axis[.side].scale.{limits,breaks,minor_breaks,expand,cap,n_breaks}. Track position: axis.<side>.position = "top"|"bottom"|"left"|"right". Route element secondary axis: map(axis.x = 2) map(axis.y = 2). Flat nested forms equivalent: aes(axis.x.line.col = "red") == aes(axis.x = aes(line = aes(col = "red"))). Track chrome: track.{background,border}.{fill,col,lwd,alpha} track.window.{background,border}.{fill,col,lwd,alpha}. Gridlines: axis.x.gridline = TRUE / axis.y.gridline = TRUE enable; style via axis.[x|y].gridline.{color, lwd, lty, alpha} inheriting axis.gridline.*. Passing aes(color, lwd, ...) value also enables.

title: Auto-Legends and Gradient Legends
code: library(SeqPlotR) #>  #> Attaching package: 'SeqPlotR' #> The following object is masked from 'package:base': #>  #>     %||% library(GenomicRanges) #> Loading required package: stats4 #> Loading required package: BiocGenerics #> Loading required package: generics #>  #> Attaching package: 'generics' #> The following objects are masked from 'package:base': #>  #>     as.difftime, as.factor, as.ordered, intersect, is.element, setdiff, #>     setequal, union #>  #> Attaching package: 'BiocGenerics' #> The following objects are masked from 'package:stats': #>  #>     IQR, mad, sd, var, xtabs #> The following objects are masked from 'package:base': #>  #>     anyDuplicated, aperm, append, as.data.frame, basename, cbind, #>     colnames, dirname, do.call, duplicated, eval, evalq, Filter, Find, #>     get, grep, grepl, is.unsorted, lapply, Map, mapply, match, mget, #>     order, paste, pmax, pmax.int, pmin, pmin.int, Position, rank, #>     rbind, Reduce, rownames, sapply, saveRDS, table, tapply, unique, #>     unsplit, which.max, which.min #> Loading required package: S4Vectors #>  #> Attaching package: 'S4Vectors' #> The following object is masked from 'package:utils': #>  #>     findMatches #> The following objects are masked from 'package:base': #>  #>     expand.grid, I, unname #> Loading required package: IRanges #> Loading required package: Seqinfo  win  <- GRanges("chr1", IRanges(1, 1e6))  # 400 random genomic features with a continuous score and a discrete type label n <- 400 starts <- sort(sample(1:999000, n)) gr <- GRanges(   "chr1",   IRanges(starts, width = sample(500:3000, n, replace = TRUE)),   score  = round(runif(n, 0, 1), 3),   type   = sample(c("H3K27ac", "H3K4me3", "H3K27me3"), n, replace = TRUE) )  # Hi-C-like square matrix for the heatmap example n_bins <- 60 starts_hic <- seq(1, 1e6, length.out = n_bins + 1)[-(n_bins + 1)]  pairs    <- expand.grid(i = seq_len(n_bins), j = seq_len(n_bins)) pairs    <- pairs[pairs$j >= pairs$i, ] distance <- pairs$j - pairs$i gr_tiles <- GRanges(   "chr1",   IRanges(starts_hic[pairs$i], width = 1e6 / n_bins),   y    = pairs$j,   fill = round(exp(-distance * 0.08) + rnorm(nrow(pairs), 0, 0.05), 3) ) gr_tiles$fill <- pmax(0, pmin(1, gr_tiles$fill))  # A helper aesthetics object used throughout to open a right-side margin # wide enough for the auto-generated legend to render in. right_margin <- aes(margins = list(top = 0, right = 0.25, bottom = 0, left = 0))

title: Auto-Legends and Gradient Legends
text: Map color type column. type character vector, SeqPlotR automatically assigns one flexoki color per unique level. legend appears right track margin: Three-level discrete color legend right track margin. legend title taken column name. seq_legend() call needed.
code: p1 <- seq_plot(aesthetics = right_margin) %+%   seq_track(data = gr, mapping = map(x = start, y = score), windows = win) %+%   seq_point(     aesthetics = aes(size = 0.6, alpha = 0.7),     mapping    = map(color = type)   ) p1$plot()

title: Auto-Legends and Gradient Legends
text: map(shape = ...) works way discrete columns. Levels cycled circle → square → triangle → diamond, matching keyed legend appears right margin: Shape legend auto-generated map(shape = type) right track margin.
code: p2 <- seq_plot(aesthetics = right_margin) %+%   seq_track(data = gr, mapping = map(x = start, y = score), windows = win) %+%   seq_point(     aesthetics = aes(size = 0.8, alpha = 0.7),     mapping    = map(shape = type)   ) p2$plot()

title: Auto-Legends and Gradient Legends
text: mapped column numeric, SeqPlotR produces GradientLegendSpec draws color bar right margin. viridis palette used default. Tick positions placed automatically pretty(): Viridis color bar auto tick axis right track margin.
code: p3 <- seq_plot(aesthetics = right_margin) %+%   seq_track(data = gr, mapping = map(x = start, y = score), windows = win) %+%   seq_point(     aesthetics = aes(size = 0.7, alpha = 0.8),     mapping    = map(color = score)   ) p3$plot()

what: 4. Bar chart with discrete fill legend (seq_bar)
title: Auto-Legends and Gradient Legends
text: seq_bar supports auto-legend machinery. Map fill discrete column group gets color key right margin. fill values also automatically scaled valid colors bars render correctly: Bar chart discrete fill auto-legend map(fill = type). Map fill numeric column color-bar legend produced instead: Bar chart continuous fill gradient auto-legend.
code: p4 <- seq_plot(aesthetics = right_margin) %+%   seq_track(data = gr, mapping = map(x = start, y = score), windows = win) %+%   seq_bar(     aesthetics = aes(alpha = 0.85),     mapping    = map(x = start, y = score, fill = type)   ) p4$plot() p4b <- seq_plot(aesthetics = right_margin) %+%   seq_track(data = gr, mapping = map(x = start, y = score), windows = win) %+%   seq_bar(     aesthetics = aes(alpha = 0.85),     mapping    = map(x = start, y = score, fill = score)   ) p4b$plot()

title: Auto-Legends and Gradient Legends
text: seq_tile also supports auto-scaling. Map fill numeric column gradient legend tick axis produced automatically: Contact-map tiles auto gradient legend.
code: p7 <- seq_plot(   aesthetics = aes(margins = list(top = 0.04, bottom = 0.04,                                    left = 0.04, right = 0.25)) ) %+%   seq_track(windows = win) %+%   seq_tile(     data    = gr_tiles,     mapping = map(x = start, y = y, fill = fill)   ) p7$plot()

title: Auto-Legends and Gradient Legends
text: need control — different palette, specific limits, custom tick positions, specific placement — use seq_gradient_legend() directly attach element via legend field.

what: Continuous color bar with a custom palette
title: Auto-Legends and Gradient Legends
text: breaks = NULL (default) places ticks automatically via pretty(). Plasma color bar placed inside panel custom position.
code: gleg <- seq_gradient_legend(   palette  = "plasma",   limits   = c(0, 1),   title    = "Score",   x        = 0.75, y = 0.95,   position = "inside" )  p8 <- seq_plot() %+%   seq_track(data = gr, mapping = map(x = start, y = score), windows = win) %+%   seq_point(     mapping    = map(color = score),     aesthetics = aes(size = 0.7, alpha = 0.8),     legend     = gleg   ) p8$plot()

title: Auto-Legends and Gradient Legends
text: Set breaks integer n place n ticks pretty(), supply numeric vector exact tick positions. modes render continuous color bar tick+label axis: Color bar placed inside 5 evenly-spaced ticks. Explicit tick values: Reds color bar ticks 0.2, 0.5, 0.8.
code: gleg_breaks <- seq_gradient_legend(   palette     = "viridis",   limits      = c(0, 1),   title       = "Score",   breaks      = 5,   orientation = "vertical",   x           = 0.02, y = 0.95,   position    = "inside" )  p9 <- seq_plot() %+%   seq_track(data = gr, mapping = map(x = start, y = score), windows = win) %+%   seq_point(     mapping    = map(color = score),     aesthetics = aes(size = 0.7, alpha = 0.8),     legend     = gleg_breaks   ) p9$plot() gleg_vals <- seq_gradient_legend(   palette  = "reds",   limits   = c(0, 1),   title    = "Score",   breaks   = c(0.2, 0.5, 0.8),   x        = 0.02, y = 0.95,   position = "inside" )  p10 <- seq_plot() %+%   seq_track(data = gr, mapping = map(x = start, y = score), windows = win) %+%   seq_point(     mapping    = map(color = score),     aesthetics = aes(size = 0.7, alpha = 0.8),     legend     = gleg_vals   ) p10$plot()

title: Auto-Legends and Gradient Legends
text: Attach seq_gradient_legend() breaks seq_tile element get labelled color bar contact-map style heatmap: Contact-map heatmap four-tick gradient legend placed inside.
code: hleg <- seq_gradient_legend(   palette     = "reds",   limits      = c(0, 1),   title       = "Contact\nfrequency",   breaks      = 4,   x           = 0.72, y = 0.95,   orientation = "vertical",   position    = "inside" )  p11 <- seq_plot(   aesthetics = aes(margins = list(top = 0.04, bottom = 0.04,                                    left = 0.04, right = 0.04)) ) %+%   seq_track(windows = win) %+%   seq_tile(     data    = gr_tiles,     mapping = map(x = start, y = y, fill = fill),     legend  = hleg   ) p11$plot()

title: Auto-Legends and Gradient Legends
text: Auto-legends respect suppression hierarchy manual legends. show_legend = FALSE element suppresses auto-legend generation: seq_plot(legend = FALSE) suppresses legends including auto-legends: legend = FALSE suppresses auto-generated discrete legend.
code: el_hidden <- seq_point(   data        = gr,   mapping     = map(x = start, y = score, color = type),   show_legend = FALSE ) # auto_legend is NULL — nothing was generated is.null(el_hidden$auto_legend) #> [1] TRUE p_none <- seq_plot(legend = FALSE, aesthetics = right_margin) %+%   seq_track(data = gr, mapping = map(x = start, y = score), windows = win) %+%   seq_point(     mapping    = map(color = type),     aesthetics = aes(size = 0.6, alpha = 0.7)   ) p_none$plot()
