title: SeqPlotR Basics: Composite Elements
text: SeqPlotR automatically selects x-axis unit narrowest window track: windows track share unit axis break intervals consistent across panels. Override per-track seq_track(window_scale = ...), per-window setting mcols(windows)$scale directly (highest priority). examples use 2 Mb windows, auto-infer Mb unit.
code: library(SeqPlotR) #>  #> Attaching package: 'SeqPlotR' #> The following object is masked from 'package:base': #>  #>     %||% library(GenomicRanges) #> Loading required package: stats4 #> Loading required package: BiocGenerics #> Loading required package: generics #>  #> Attaching package: 'generics' #> The following objects are masked from 'package:base': #>  #>     as.difftime, as.factor, as.ordered, intersect, is.element, setdiff, #>     setequal, union #>  #> Attaching package: 'BiocGenerics' #> The following objects are masked from 'package:stats': #>  #>     IQR, mad, sd, var, xtabs #> The following objects are masked from 'package:base': #>  #>     anyDuplicated, aperm, append, as.data.frame, basename, cbind, #>     colnames, dirname, do.call, duplicated, eval, evalq, Filter, Find, #>     get, grep, grepl, is.unsorted, lapply, Map, mapply, match, mget, #>     order, paste, pmax, pmax.int, pmin, pmin.int, Position, rank, #>     rbind, Reduce, rownames, sapply, saveRDS, table, tapply, unique, #>     unsplit, which.max, which.min #> Loading required package: S4Vectors #>  #> Attaching package: 'S4Vectors' #> The following object is masked from 'package:utils': #>  #>     findMatches #> The following objects are masked from 'package:base': #>  #>     expand.grid, I, unname #> Loading required package: IRanges #> Loading required package: Seqinfo  # A single 2-Mb window on chr1 → axis labels read as 1.0, 1.5, 2.0 Mb. win <- GRanges("chr1", IRanges(1e6, 3e6))

title: SeqPlotR Basics: Composite Elements
text: Every track allocated rectangular cell page layout partitioned five nested zones, two track level three window level: Track outer margin — outermost band track cell. Axis titles (taken track mapping) drawn . seq_track(track_outer_margin = ...). Default 0.02. Track inner margin — separates title band window row. seq_track(track_inner_margin = ...). Default 0.02. Window outer margin — per-window spacer inside track plot region. seq_track(window_outer_margin = ...). Default 0. Window inner margin — per-window band holding axis lines, ticks, tick labels. seq_track(window_inner_margin = ...). Default 0.02. Plot area — remains per window. Elements render ; panel$inner carries coordinates. Axis lines sit boundary plot area window inner margin. gap adjacent windows plot- track-level aes("window.gap.width" = ...) (default 0.01); gap tracks plot-level trackGaps (default 0.01). old seq_track(window_margin = ...) constructor argument deprecated — see Window gap width . schematic lays two tracks × two windows using nothing SeqPlotR, zone coloured via plot-level trackBackground, trackInnerBackground, windowBoxBackground, windowInnerBackground, windowBackground aesthetics. seq_text places label plot area.  Reading schematic, outside inside track: Pink — track outer margin (axis titles live ). Yellow — track inner margin. Purple — window outer margin (per-window spacer). Green — window inner margin (axis lines, ticks, tick labels live ). Blue — plot area, elements render. vertical green/blue split inside track window margin two windows. horizontal gap two tracks track gap. next chunk shows layout real elements axes . Axis titles appear track outer margin, pulling text directly track mapping (x title start, y titles score impact).
code: # Two windows (2 Mb and 1.5 Mb wide). schematic_win <- GRanges(   "chr1", IRanges(start = c(1e6, 5e6), end = c(3e6, 6.5e6)))  # One label centred inside each window for each track. mk_labels <- function(track_name) {   GRanges(     "chr1", IRanges(start = c(2e6, 5.75e6), width = 1),     label = paste0(track_name, "\nWindow ", c(1, 2))   ) } lbl_A <- mk_labels("Track A") lbl_B <- mk_labels("Track B")  # Zone colors via plot-level aesthetics; axes turned off so the five # nested rectangles are visible without interference. schematic_aes <- aes(   trackBackground        = "#F89A8A",  # track outer margin   trackBorder            = "grey30",   trackInnerBackground   = "#ECCB60",  # track inner margin   trackInnerBorder       = "grey30",   windowBoxBackground    = "#A699D0",  # window outer margin   windowBoxBorder        = "grey30",   windowInnerBackground  = "#BEC97E",  # window inner margin   windowInnerBorder      = "grey30",   windowBackground       = "#92BFDB",  # plot area   windowBorder           = "grey30",   xAxisLine = FALSE, xAxisTicks = FALSE, xAxisLabels = FALSE,   xAxisTitle = FALSE,   yAxisLine = FALSE, yAxisTicks = FALSE, yAxisLabels = FALSE,   yAxisTitle = FALSE,   trackGaps = 0.04,   "window.gap.width" = 0.03 )  seq_plot(aesthetics = schematic_aes) %|%   seq_track(track_id = "A",             data = lbl_A,             mapping = map(x = start, label = label),             windows = schematic_win,             track_outer_margin  = 0.035,             track_inner_margin  = 0.025,             window_outer_margin = 0.025,             window_inner_margin = 0.035) %+%   seq_text(aesthetics = aes(fontsize = 12, color = "#1C1B1A")) %__%   seq_track(track_id = "B",             data = lbl_B,             mapping = map(x = start, label = label),             windows = schematic_win,             track_outer_margin  = 0.035,             track_inner_margin  = 0.025,             window_outer_margin = 0.025,             window_inner_margin = 0.035) %+%   seq_text(aesthetics = aes(fontsize = 12, color = "#1C1B1A")) -> p p$plot() trkA_gr <- GRanges("chr1",   IRanges(start = c(1.2e6, 1.8e6, 2.4e6, 5.2e6, 5.7e6, 6.2e6), width = 1e5),   score = c(0.3, 0.7, 0.5, 0.6, 0.4, 0.8))  trkB_gr <- GRanges("chr1",   IRanges(start = c(1.3e6, 2.0e6, 2.7e6, 5.3e6, 5.9e6, 6.3e6), width = 1),   impact = c(0.5, 0.9, 0.4, 0.7, 0.3, 0.6))  real_aes <- aes(   trackBackground      = "#FCEBEA",   trackInnerBackground = "#FAF5DC",   "window.gap.width"   = 0.02 )  seq_plot(aesthetics = real_aes) %|%   seq_track(track_id = "Track A",             data = trkA_gr,             mapping = map(x = start, y = score),             windows = schematic_win,             track_outer_margin = 0.03,             window_inner_margin = 0.04) %+%   seq_bar(aesthetics = aes(fill = "#4385BE")) %__%   seq_track(track_id = "Track B",             data = trkB_gr,             mapping = map(x = start, y = impact),             windows = schematic_win,             track_outer_margin = 0.03,             window_inner_margin = 0.04) %+%   seq_lollipop(aesthetics = aes(color = "#AF3029", linewidth = 1.2)) -> p p$plot()

title: SeqPlotR Basics: Composite Elements
text: aes("window.gap.width" = <npc>) controls horizontal gap adjacent windows within track. Set seq_plot() apply across every track, specific seq_track() override plot-level value track . Default 0.01.  legacy seq_track(window_margin = ...) constructor argument deprecated emits warning used; switch aes("window.gap.width" = ...). older aes(window_gaps = ...) key still works alias window.gap.width takes priority set.
code: gap_win <- GRanges(   "chr1",   IRanges(start = c(1, 1e6 + 1, 2e6 + 1), width = 1e6) ) gap_lbl <- function(track_name) {   GRanges(     "chr1",     IRanges(start = c(5e5, 1.5e6, 2.5e6), width = 1),     label = paste0(track_name, "\nW", c(1, 2, 3))   ) }  # Three tracks, three gap widths: collapsed, default, generous. seq_plot(aesthetics = aes(     windowBackground = "#92BFDB", windowBorder = "grey30",     xAxisLine = FALSE, xAxisTicks = FALSE, xAxisLabels = FALSE,     xAxisTitle = FALSE, yAxisLine = FALSE, yAxisTicks = FALSE,     yAxisLabels = FALSE, yAxisTitle = FALSE,     "window.gap.width" = 0   # plot-level default for all tracks   )) %|%   seq_track(track_id = "Collapsed (0)",             data = gap_lbl("Collapsed"),             mapping = map(x = start, label = label),             windows = gap_win) %+%   seq_text(aesthetics = aes(fontsize = 11)) %__%   seq_track(track_id = "Default (0.01)",             data = gap_lbl("Default"),             mapping = map(x = start, label = label),             windows = gap_win,             aesthetics = aes("window.gap.width" = 0.01)) %+%   seq_text(aesthetics = aes(fontsize = 11)) %__%   seq_track(track_id = "Generous (0.05)",             data = gap_lbl("Generous"),             mapping = map(x = start, label = label),             windows = gap_win,             aesthetics = aes("window.gap.width" = 0.05)) %+%   seq_text(aesthetics = aes(fontsize = 11)) -> p p$plot()

title: SeqPlotR Basics: Composite Elements
text: five zones independent knobs. examples share single small dataset vary margin arguments effect one visible. Minimal: drop axis titles track outer margin — plot fills full track cell.  Room titles labels: widen track window margins. axis titles (start, score) auto-populate mapping.  Asymmetric margins: length-4 form uses base-R par(mar = c(bottom, left, top, right)) order. x-axis title gets wider bottom band; top edge stays flush.  Window inner vs window outer margin: turn windowBoxBackground windowInnerBackground see two per-window bands. window outer margin (purple) optional spacer; window inner margin (green) ticks labels sit.
code: recipe_win <- GRanges("chr1", IRanges(1e6, 3e6)) recipe_gr  <- GRanges(   "chr1",   IRanges(start = seq(1.1e6, 2.9e6, by = 2e5), width = 8e4),   score = runif(10, 0.2, 1.0) ) seq_plot(aesthetics = aes(xAxisTitle = FALSE, yAxisTitle = FALSE)) %|%   seq_track(data = recipe_gr,             mapping = map(x = start, y = score),             windows = recipe_win,             track_outer_margin = 0,             window_inner_margin = 0.02) %+%   seq_bar(aesthetics = aes(fill = "#4385BE")) -> p p$plot() seq_plot() %|%   seq_track(data = recipe_gr,             mapping = map(x = start, y = score),             windows = recipe_win,             track_outer_margin = c(0.05, 0.05, 0.02, 0.02),             window_inner_margin = 0.06) %+%   seq_bar(aesthetics = aes(fill = "#4385BE")) -> p p$plot() seq_plot() %|%   seq_track(data = recipe_gr,             mapping = map(x = start, y = score),             windows = recipe_win,             track_outer_margin = c(0.06, 0.06, 0, 0),             track_inner_margin = 0.015,             window_inner_margin = 0.04) %+%   seq_bar(aesthetics = aes(fill = "#4385BE")) -> p p$plot() seq_plot(aesthetics = aes(     windowBoxBackground   = "#C4B9E0",     windowInnerBackground = "#BEC97E"   )) %|%   seq_track(data = recipe_gr,             mapping = map(x = start, y = score),             windows = recipe_win,             track_outer_margin  = 0.03,             track_inner_margin  = 0.01,             window_outer_margin = 0.02,             window_inner_margin = 0.05) %+%   seq_bar(aesthetics = aes(fill = "#4385BE")) -> p p$plot()

title: SeqPlotR Basics: Composite Elements
text: Relative sizing controlled three arguments: track_width (within row), track_height (across rows), scale mcols column windows GRanges (within track’s plot region). Unequal track widths one row. Track 3× wider B.  Unequal track heights stacked rows. points track twice tall bar track .  Per-window scale factor. Within multi-window track, windows sized width(windows) * mcols(windows)$scale. Defaults auto-inferred scale windows. Setting one window’s scale 0 shrinks nothing; setting distinct scales compresses expands individual windows relative others.  Track-level unit override window_scale. Pass window_scale seq_track force fixed scale factor windows, positional vector set window independently. window_scale overrides auto-inferred unit overridden mcols(windows)$scale already present. Length-1 applies windows; mismatched length triggers warning recycles.
code: win_small <- GRanges("chr1", IRanges(1e6, 3e6)) gr_small  <- GRanges("chr1",   IRanges(start = seq(1.1e6, 2.9e6, by = 2e5), width = 8e4),   score = runif(10, 0.2, 1.0))  seq_plot() %|%   seq_track(track_id = "A",             data = gr_small,             mapping = map(x = start, y = score),             windows = win_small,             track_width = 3) %+%   seq_bar(aesthetics = aes(fill = "#4385BE")) %|%   seq_track(track_id = "B",             data = gr_small,             mapping = map(x = start, y = score),             windows = win_small,             track_width = 1) %+%   seq_lollipop(aesthetics = aes(color = "#AF3029")) -> p p$plot() xs_sig <- seq(1.05e6, 2.95e6, length.out = 40) sig_gr <- GRanges("chr1", IRanges(start = xs_sig, width = 1),                   score = sin((xs_sig - 1e6) / 3e5) * 0.4 + 0.5)  seq_plot() %|%   seq_track(track_id = "Signal",             data = sig_gr,             mapping = map(x = start, y = score),             windows = win_small,             track_height = 2) %+%   seq_line(aesthetics = aes(color = "#4385BE", linewidth = 1.5)) %__%   seq_track(track_id = "Bars",             data = gr_small,             mapping = map(x = start, y = score),             windows = win_small,             track_height = 1) %+%   seq_bar(aesthetics = aes(fill = "#8B7EC8")) -> p p$plot() # Two windows of equal genomic width, but the second rendered at # 2× the first's relative width. scaled_win <- GRanges(   "chr1", IRanges(start = c(1e6, 5e6), end = c(3e6, 7e6))) S4Vectors::mcols(scaled_win)$scale <- c(1e-6, 2e-6)  scaled_gr <- GRanges("chr1",   IRanges(start = c(seq(1.2e6, 2.8e6, length.out = 5),                     seq(5.2e6, 6.8e6, length.out = 5)),           width = 1e5),   score = runif(10, 0.2, 1.0))  seq_plot() %|%   seq_track(data = scaled_gr,             mapping = map(x = start, y = score),             windows = scaled_win) %+%   seq_bar(aesthetics = aes(fill = "#4385BE")) -> p p$plot() # Two 5 Mb windows — auto-inference gives Mb, but we force kb. mb_win <- GRanges("chr1", IRanges(start = c(1e6, 1e7 + 1), width = 5e6)) mb_gr  <- GRanges("chr1",   IRanges(start = c(seq(1.2e6, 5.8e6, length.out = 5),                     seq(1.02e7, 1.58e7, length.out = 5)),           width = 2e5),   score = runif(10, 0.2, 1.0))  seq_plot() %|%   seq_track(data = mb_gr,             mapping = map(x = start, y = score),             windows = mb_win,             window_scale = 1e-3) %+%   seq_bar(aesthetics = aes(fill = "#4385BE")) -> p p$plot()

title: SeqPlotR Basics: Composite Elements
text: One filled rectangle per interval. group mapping, bar’s height mapped y value.
code: bar_gr <- GRanges(   "chr1",   IRanges(start = seq(1.1e6, 2.9e6, by = 2e5), width = 8e4),   score = runif(10, 0.2, 1.0) )  seq_plot() %|%   seq_track(data = bar_gr,             mapping = map(x = start, y = score),             windows = win) %+%   seq_bar() -> p p$plot()

title: SeqPlotR Basics: Composite Elements
text: Supplying group mapping causes bars identical x positions stack. Colors drawn Flexoki palette keyed group level.
code: stack_gr <- GRanges(   "chr1",   IRanges(start = rep(seq(1.2e6, 2.8e6, by = 2e5), each = 3), width = 5e4),   value    = runif(27, 0.1, 0.5),   category = rep(c("A", "B", "C"), times = 9) )  seq_plot() %|%   seq_track(data = stack_gr,             mapping = map(x = start, y = value, group = category),             windows = win) %+%   seq_bar() -> p p$plot() #> 8 out-of-bounds data points excluded! (seq_bar)

what: seq_ribbon — filled band between two y series
title: SeqPlotR Basics: Composite Elements
text: Requires y_min y_max mappings. Useful confidence bands.
code: xs <- seq(1.05e6, 2.95e6, length.out = 50) mu <- sin((xs - 1e6) / 3e5) * 0.3 + 0.5 band <- 0.15  ribbon_gr <- GRanges(   "chr1", IRanges(start = xs, width = 1),   mean = mu,   lo   = mu - band,   hi   = mu + band )  seq_plot() %|%   seq_track(data = ribbon_gr,             mapping = map(x = start, y_min = lo, y_max = hi),             windows = win) %+%   seq_ribbon(aesthetics = aes(fill = "#4385BE", alpha = 0.5)) -> p p$plot()

title: SeqPlotR Basics: Composite Elements
text: Computes stats::density() mapped y renders distribution filled area. density evaluation axis runs horizontally, mapped track’s yscale.
code: dens_gr <- GRanges(   "chr1", IRanges(start = seq(1e6, 3e6, length.out = 200), width = 1),   score = c(rnorm(120, mean = 0.3, sd = 0.05),             rnorm(80,  mean = 0.7, sd = 0.08)) )  seq_plot() %|%   seq_track(data = dens_gr,             mapping = map(x = start, y = score),             windows = win) %+%   seq_density(aesthetics = aes(fill = "#879A39", alpha = 0.7)) -> p p$plot()

title: SeqPlotR Basics: Composite Elements
code: tile_gr <- GRanges(   "chr1",   IRanges(start = seq(1.1e6, 2.9e6, by = 1e5), width = 8e4),   fill_col = sample(flexoki_palette(5), 19, replace = TRUE) )  seq_plot() %|%   seq_track(data = tile_gr,             mapping = map(x = start, fill = fill_col),             windows = win) %+%   seq_tile(aesthetics = aes(rotate = FALSE)) -> p p$plot()

title: SeqPlotR Basics: Composite Elements
text: Vertical stem baseline (default 0) y, point y. Good sparse, discrete events (e.g. mutation calls).
code: lp_gr <- GRanges(   "chr1",   IRanges(start = sample(seq(1.05e6, 2.95e6, by = 1e4), 15), width = 1),   impact = runif(15, 0.2, 1.0) )  seq_plot() %|%   seq_track(data = lp_gr,             mapping = map(x = start, y = impact),             windows = win) %+%   seq_lollipop(aesthetics = aes(color = "#AF3029", linewidth = 1.2)) -> p p$plot()

title: SeqPlotR Basics: Composite Elements
text: column references come map(): group joins features one gene, strand orients arrows, type distinguishes exons UTRs, label places gene name, color tints gene.
code: gene_gr <- GRanges(   "chr1",   IRanges(     start = c(1.10e6, 1.20e6, 1.35e6, 1.50e6, 1.58e6,               1.90e6, 2.05e6, 2.18e6, 2.30e6,               2.55e6, 2.68e6, 2.80e6),     width = c( 3e4,  6e4,  4e4,  5e4,  2e4,                4e4,  8e4,  5e4,  3e4,                6e4,  3e4,  5e4)   ),   gene_id   = c("A","A","A","A","A",                 "B","B","B","B",                 "C","C","C"),   gene_name = c("TP53","TP53","TP53","TP53","TP53",                 "MYC","MYC","MYC","MYC",                 "BRCA1","BRCA1","BRCA1"),   strand_col = c("+","+","+","+","+",                  "-","-","-","-",                  "+","+","+"),   feature = c("UTR","exon","exon","exon","UTR",               "UTR","exon","exon","UTR",               "exon","exon","exon"),   color = c(rep("#205EA6", 5),             rep("#AF3029", 4),             rep("#66800B", 3)) )  seq_plot() %|%   seq_track(data = gene_gr,             mapping = map(group  = gene_id,                           type   = feature,                           strand = strand_col,                           label  = gene_name,                           color  = color),             windows = win) %+%   seq_gene(map(group  = gene_id,                type   = feature,                strand = strand_col,                label  = gene_name,                color  = color)) -> p p$plot()

title: SeqPlotR Basics: Composite Elements
text: single windows GRanges may contain several ranges. window becomes panel within track, relative widths set width(windows). mapping resolves every window independently, per-panel x-scales reflect local coordinates.
code: multi_win <- GRanges(   "chr1",   IRanges(start = c(1.0e6, 5.0e6), end = c(3.0e6, 6.5e6)) )  starts_A <- seq(1.1e6, 2.9e6, length.out = 10)  # 10 in window 1 starts_B <- seq(5.1e6, 6.4e6, length.out = 10)  # 10 in window 2 multi_gr <- GRanges(   "chr1",   IRanges(start = c(starts_A, starts_B), width = 5e4),   score  = runif(20, 0.2, 1.0),   region = rep(c("Region A", "Region B"), times = c(10, 10)) )  seq_plot() %|%   seq_track(data = multi_gr,             mapping = map(x = start, y = score, group = region),             windows = multi_win) %+%   seq_bar() -> p p$plot()

what: Flipping the axes: genomic on x vs. genomic on y
title: SeqPlotR Basics: Composite Elements
text: default every seq_track runs genomic x along width lets elements choose y-axis scale data. Pass scale_y = seq_scale_genomic(...) flip track genomic position runs along y instead; set scale_x = seq_scale_continuous(...) (seq_scale_discrete(...)) carry scalar / categorical value x-axis. data can rendered either way. seq_track() exposes three arguments drive : scale_x — controls x-axis scale (defaults genomic range track’s windows). scale_y — controls y-axis scale. seq_scale_genomic(...) auto-enables uses_genomic_y. y_windows — shorthand set genomic y-range without constructing scale object.

previous_headings: Flipping the axes: genomic on x vs. genomic on y
title: SeqPlotR Basics: Composite Elements
text: small gene-level table (log2fc per gene, one point per gene): Genomic x, scalar y (conventional). x = start runs along 1–3 Mb genomic window; y = log2fc scalar.  Scalar x, genomic y (flipped). scale_x carries log2fc [-3, 3]; scale_y = seq_scale_genomic(win) puts genomic position vertical axis. lollipops now fan horizontally x = 0, one row per gene true genomic coordinate.  two plots encode table — orientation genomic axis changes. flipped version y-tick labels fall back Mb units (genomic yScaleFactor, matching x-axis default) 1.5 Mb reads 1.5 rather 1,500,000.
code: gene_meta <- GRanges("chr1",   IRanges(start = seq(1.1e6, 2.9e6, length.out = 14), width = 1e4),   log2fc = c(-2.4, -1.3, 0.9, 2.1,  1.6, -0.5,  2.7,               0.4, -1.8,  1.2, 0.2,  0.8, -2.2,  1.1),   sig    = c("down","down","ns","up","up","ns","up",              "ns","down","up","ns","ns","down","up"))  sig_cols <- c(up = "#AF3029", down = "#205EA6", ns = "#878580") seq_plot() %|%   seq_track(data = gene_meta,             mapping = map(x = start, y = log2fc, color = sig),             windows = win) %+%   seq_segment(mapping = map(x = start, x_end = start,                             y = 0, y_end = log2fc, color = sig),               aesthetics = aes(linewidth = 2)) %+%   seq_point(aesthetics = aes(size = 0.9)) -> p p$plot() #> 14 out-of-bounds data points excluded! (seq_point) seq_plot() %|%   seq_track(data = gene_meta,             mapping = map(x = log2fc, y = mid, color = sig),             windows = win,             scale_x = seq_scale_continuous(limits = c(-3, 3)),             scale_y = seq_scale_genomic(win),             track_width = 0.6) %+%   seq_segment(mapping = map(x = 0, x_end = log2fc,                             y = mid, y_end = mid, color = sig),               aesthetics = aes(linewidth = 2)) %+%   seq_point(aesthetics = aes(size = 0.9)) -> p p$plot()

previous_headings: Flipping the axes: genomic on x vs. genomic on y
what: Coverage signal flipped onto the y-axis
title: SeqPlotR Basics: Composite Elements
text: principle extends continuous signal. , coverage curve shown first conventional orientation, flipped depth runs along x genomic position along y — useful narrow sidebar next wider browser panel.
code: cov_xs <- seq(1.05e6, 2.95e6, length.out = 200) cov_gr <- GRanges("chr1", IRanges(cov_xs, width = 1),                   depth = 0.5 + 0.3 * sin((cov_xs - 1e6) / 3e5)) seq_plot() %|%   seq_track(data = cov_gr,             mapping = map(x = start, y = depth),             windows = win) %+%   seq_area(aesthetics = aes(fill = "#4385BE",                             color = "#205EA6",                             alpha = 0.6, linewidth = 0.7)) -> p p$plot() seq_plot() %|%   seq_track(data = cov_gr,             mapping = map(x = depth, y = start),             windows = win) %+%   seq_area(aesthetics = aes(fill = "#4385BE",                             color = "#205EA6",                             alpha = 0.6, linewidth = 0.7)) -> p p$plot()

previous_headings: Flipping the axes: genomic on x vs. genomic on y
title: SeqPlotR Basics: Composite Elements
text: Rotated tiles (aes(rotate = TRUE)) place observation diamond (genomic_x, genomic_y) space — natural representation Hi-C contacts 2D genomic relationship. axes genomic; pass seq_scale_genomic() scale_y built distance-bin GRanges.
code: n_contacts <- 60 hic_win <- GRanges("chr1", IRanges(1e6, 3e6))  # Toy Hi-C: pick x-bins, then y-bins offset by a genomic distance. x_starts <- sample(seq(1.05e6, 2.95e6, by = 2e4), n_contacts, replace = TRUE) y_offset <- sample(seq(2e4, 8e5, by = 2e4), n_contacts, replace = TRUE)  x_gr <- GRanges("chr1", IRanges(start = x_starts,            width = 2e4),                 score = runif(n_contacts)) y_gr <- GRanges("chr1", IRanges(start = x_starts + y_offset, width = 2e4))  # Map [0,1] scores → a soft red ramp for the fill column. score_to_color <- function(s) {   pal <- grDevices::colorRampPalette(c("#F4A4C2", "#A02C6D"))(100)   pal[pmin(pmax(round(s * 99) + 1, 1), 100)] } x_gr$fill_col <- score_to_color(x_gr$score)  seq_plot() %|%   seq_track(data = x_gr,             mapping = map(x = start, fill = fill_col),             windows = hic_win,             scale_y = seq_scale_genomic(               GRanges("chr1", IRanges(0, max(y_offset) + 2e4)))) %+%   seq_tile(data2 = y_gr, aesthetics = aes(rotate = TRUE)) -> p p$plot()

title: SeqPlotR Basics: Composite Elements
text: Stack several tracks one plot mix composite types. track keeps data, mapping, y-scale.
code: seq_plot() %|%   seq_track(track_id = "Signal",             data = ribbon_gr,             mapping = map(x = start, y_min = lo, y_max = hi),             windows = win) %+%   seq_ribbon(aesthetics = aes(fill = "#4385BE", alpha = 0.5)) %__%   seq_track(track_id = "Bars",             data = bar_gr,             mapping = map(x = start, y = score),             windows = win) %+%   seq_bar(aesthetics = aes(fill = "#8B7EC8")) %__%   seq_track(track_id = "Genes",             data = gene_gr,             mapping = map(group  = gene_id,                           type   = feature,                           strand = strand_col,                           label  = gene_name,                           color  = color),             windows = win) %+%   seq_gene(map(group  = gene_id,                type   = feature,                strand = strand_col,                label  = gene_name,                color  = color)) -> p p$plot()

title: SeqPlotR Basics: Composite Elements
text: Passing layout string seq_plot(layout = ...) fixes track’s position track_id. Every letter string names track marks cells covers; # blank cell. seq_track(direction = ...) ignored mode — position driven entirely string. layout reserves top row signal ribbon density sidebar, middle row bars spans whole width, bottom row gene models. seq_preview_layout() renders layout -front can verify geometry adding data.
code: layout_str <- " AAAB CCCC DDDD " seq_preview_layout(layout = layout_str) seq_plot(layout = layout_str) %+%   seq_track(track_id = "A",             data = ribbon_gr,             mapping = map(x = start, y_min = lo, y_max = hi),             windows = win) %+%   seq_ribbon(aesthetics = aes(fill = "#4385BE", alpha = 0.5)) %+%   seq_track(track_id = "B",             data = dens_gr,             mapping = map(x = start, y = score),             windows = win) %+%   seq_density(aesthetics = aes(fill = "#879A39", alpha = 0.7)) %+%   seq_track(track_id = "C",             data = bar_gr,             mapping = map(x = start, y = score),             windows = win) %+%   seq_bar(aesthetics = aes(fill = "#8B7EC8")) %+%   seq_track(track_id = "D",             data = gene_gr,             mapping = map(group  = gene_id,                           type   = feature,                           strand = strand_col,                           label  = gene_name,                           color  = color),             windows = win) %+%   seq_gene(map(group  = gene_id,                type   = feature,                strand = strand_col,                label  = gene_name,                color  = color)) -> p p$plot()

what: A plot that spans multiple rows
title: SeqPlotR Basics: Composite Elements
text: letter’s region layout string axis-aligned bounding box, letter whose cells form contiguous rectangle can span multiple rows, multiple columns, . layout , B occupies rows right-hand column C share left column D spans full width third row.  rendered plot puts density summary (track B) alongside two stacked detail tracks (= ribbon, C = bars), genes track beneath . density track spans rows right, y-extent full height + C together.  Layout-string rules: Every non-# letter must form rectangular bounding box (SeqPlotR errors L-shape non-rectangle detected). # cells render empty space — track, axes. Tracks whose track_id present string silently skipped, set seq_track() calls can reused across different layout strings.
code: span_layout <- " AABB CCBB DDDD " seq_preview_layout(layout = span_layout) seq_plot(layout = span_layout) %+%   seq_track(track_id = "A",             data = ribbon_gr,             mapping = map(x = start, y_min = lo, y_max = hi),             windows = win) %+%   seq_ribbon(aesthetics = aes(fill = "#4385BE", alpha = 0.5)) %+%   seq_track(track_id = "B",             data = dens_gr,             mapping = map(x = start, y = score),             windows = win) %+%   seq_density(aesthetics = aes(fill = "#879A39", alpha = 0.7)) %+%   seq_track(track_id = "C",             data = bar_gr,             mapping = map(x = start, y = score),             windows = win) %+%   seq_bar(aesthetics = aes(fill = "#8B7EC8")) %+%   seq_track(track_id = "D",             data = gene_gr,             mapping = map(group  = gene_id,                           type   = feature,                           strand = strand_col,                           label  = gene_name,                           color  = color),             windows = win) %+%   seq_gene(map(group  = gene_id,                type   = feature,                strand = strand_col,                label  = gene_name,                color  = color)) -> p p$plot()

what: In-panel axis titles and range labels
title: Batch 9: In-panel axes, seq_reads, seq_scalebar, and file connections
text: default behaviour axes changed — titles render margin band per-tick labels stack along axis. Two new theme keys let override per-axis basis: axis.<dim>.title.position axis.<dim>.labels.style / .position.  y-axis lives entirely inside panel — title sits NPC (0.02, 0.95) (top-left), single bracketed range label [lo–hi] sits bottom-left corner instead per-tick labels.
code: win <- GRanges("chr1", IRanges(1, 1000)) gr  <- GRanges("chr1", IRanges(c(100, 400, 700), width = 80),                score = c(1, 3, 2))  p <- seq_plot() %+%   (seq_track(      data    = gr, windows = win,      aesthetics = aes(        "axis.y.title.text"      = "Score",        "axis.y.title.position"  = c(0.02, 0.95),        "axis.y.labels.style"    = "range",        "axis.y.labels.position" = c(0.02, 0.05)      )    ) %+%    seq_bar(map(x = start, y = score))) p$plot()

title: Batch 9: In-panel axes, seq_reads, seq_scalebar, and file connections
text: seq_gene previously placed labels strand-aware one side gene. new gene.label aes lets compose label x/y placement independently using c("start"|"end", "top"|"bottom").
code: gr_genes <- GRanges("chr1",   IRanges(start = c(100,  400, 1000, 1500),           end   = c(300,  600, 1200, 1700)),   gene_id    = c("geneA", "geneA", "geneB", "geneB"),   gene_name  = c("GeneA", "GeneA", "GeneB", "GeneB"),   strand_col = c("+", "+", "-", "-"),   feat_type  = c("exon", "exon", "exon", "exon") )  win_g <- GRanges("chr1", IRanges(1, 2000))  p <- seq_plot() %+%   (seq_track(data = gr_genes, windows = win_g) %+%    seq_gene(      map(group = gene_id, label = gene_name,          strand = strand_col, type = feat_type),      aesthetics = aes(        gene.label = aes(position = c("start", "top"),                         color    = "navy",                         size     = 0.55)      )    )) p$plot()

title: Batch 9: In-panel axes, seq_reads, seq_scalebar, and file connections
text: seq_ideogram(scope = "full") renders entire chromosome — just bands overlapping current window — overlays translucent highlight box marking sub-range. style = "rounded" adds rounded caps two telomere ends. axis labels read chromosome coordinates, give track whole chromosome windows, pass highlight_range mark sub-range interest:  telomere.radius = 1.0 produces full half-circle cap (cap depth = band height / 2 inches); smaller values give shallower curve.
code: cb <- load_cytobands() chr1_full <- GRanges("chr1", IRanges(1, 248956422)) zoom_in   <- GRanges("chr1", IRanges(1.2e8, 1.6e8))  p <- seq_plot() %+%   seq_track(windows = zoom_in, height = 1.5) %+%    seq_ideogram(cb,                 scope           = "full",                 style           = "rounded",                 highlight_range = zoom_in,                 aesthetics = aes(                   highlight       = aes(fill = "red", alpha = 0.5),                   telomere.radius = 1                 )) p$plot()

title: Batch 9: In-panel axes, seq_reads, seq_scalebar, and file connections
text: seq_scalebar() draws horizontal length reference user-controlled panel position. Useful x-axis hidden still want communicate scale.  label auto-formats bp length: 1e5 → "100 kb", 2e6 → "2 Mb", 500 → "500 bp". Override label = "...".
code: win_sb <- GRanges("chr1", IRanges(1, 1e6))  p <- seq_plot() %+%   (seq_track(      windows    = win_sb,      aesthetics = aes("axis.x1.visible" = FALSE)) %+%    seq_scalebar(length_bp = 1e5,                 hjust     = 0.95,                 vjust     = 0.5,                 bar_lwd   = 1.5)) p$plot()

title: Batch 9: In-panel axes, seq_reads, seq_scalebar, and file connections
text: open_bigwig(), open_bam(), open_hic() return lightweight S3 connection objects. probes file header construction (hundred bytes /O), records per-format max_fetch_bp guardrail, exposes $fetch(region) method pulls requested genomic span demand. guardrail fires /O — asking span wider max_fetch_bp raises informative error rather silently loading entire chromosome.
code: bw  <- open_bigwig("path/to/signal.bw") bw                       # <SeqBigWig>  ...  max_fetch_bp: 50,000,000 sig <- bw$fetch(GRanges("chr1", IRanges(1, 1e6)))   # GRanges of signal  bam <- open_bam("path/to/aln.bam") bam$fetch(GRanges("chr1", IRanges(20363000, 20370000)))  hic <- open_hic("path/to/contacts.hic", resolution = 25000) hic$fetch(GRanges("chr1", IRanges(1e7, 1.1e7))) is_seq_file_conn(list())            # FALSE #> [1] FALSE is_seq_file_conn("path/to/file.bw") # FALSE #> [1] FALSE

title: Batch 9: In-panel axes, seq_reads, seq_scalebar, and file connections
text: seq_reads() loads alignments indexed BAM construction time, packs rows insert length (start), renders chevron polygons (right-pointing + strand, left −). Mate pairs linked thin horizontal line default. read becomes one chevron; mate pairs share single packed row connected outer extent. max_width guardrail (default 100 kb) fires BAM /O window wide. sample BAM available, call renders IGV-style row- packed view underlying alignments:
code: win  <- GRanges("chr1", IRanges(20363000, 20370000)) reads <- seq_reads("path/to/aln.bam", win,                    sort_by    = "insert_length",                    link_mates = TRUE)  seq_plot() %+%   (seq_track(windows = win, height = 4) %+% reads) win_reads <- GRanges("chr20", IRanges(30000000, 30050000)) reads <- seq_reads(demo_bam, win_reads, max_reads = 500)  p <- seq_plot() %+%   (seq_track(windows = win_reads, height = 4) %+% reads) p$plot()

title: SeqPlotR Elements: Primitives, Composites, and Ideograms
text: Every primitive shares three-stage contract (initialize → prep → draw) shorthand: seq_<name>(map(...), aesthetics = aes(...)). differs map() vocabulary one consumes. Per-track data + mapping inherited, single seq_track() can host several primitives drawing table.

title: SeqPlotR Elements: Primitives, Composites, and Ideograms
text: Required map() fields: x, y. Optional: color, fill, size, shape, alpha.
code: xs       <- seq(1.05e6, 2.95e6, length.out = 40) point_gr <- GRanges("chr1", IRanges(xs, width = 1),                     score = sin((xs - 1e6) / 5e5) + rnorm(40, 0, 0.12),                     cat   = sample(c("A", "B", "C"), 40, replace = TRUE))  seq_plot() %|%   seq_track(data = point_gr,             mapping = map(x = start, y = score),             windows = win) %+%   seq_point(map(color = cat),             aesthetics = aes(size = 0.7, alpha = 0.9)) -> p p$plot()

title: SeqPlotR Elements: Primitives, Composites, and Ideograms
text: Required: x, y. aes(type = "step") switches step line (useful signal tracks represent piecewise-constant values).
code: line_xs <- seq(1.05e6, 2.95e6, length.out = 100) line_gr <- GRanges("chr1", IRanges(line_xs, width = 1),                    signal = sin((line_xs - 1e6) / 3e5) * 0.4 + 0.5,                    step   = round(sin((line_xs - 1e6) / 3e5) * 2) / 4 + 0.5)  seq_plot() %|%   seq_track(track_id = "Smooth",             data = line_gr,             mapping = map(x = start, y = signal),             windows = win) %+%   seq_line(aesthetics = aes(color = "#205EA6", linewidth = 1.4)) %|%   seq_track(track_id = "Step",             data = line_gr,             mapping = map(x = start, y = step),             windows = win) %+%   seq_line(aesthetics = aes(color = "#AF3029",                             linewidth = 1.4,                             type = "step")) -> p p$plot()

what: seq_segment — one straight line per row
title: SeqPlotR Elements: Primitives, Composites, and Ideograms
text: Required: x, x_end, y, y_end. Great quick interval marks — peak span, read pair, Manhattan-style baseline.
code: seg_gr <- GRanges("chr1",   IRanges(start = seq(1.1e6, 2.9e6, by = 2.5e5), width = 1),   x_end = seq(1.1e6, 2.9e6, by = 2.5e5) + 1.5e5,   y     = runif(8, 0.2, 0.6),   y_end = runif(8, 0.4, 0.9),   grp   = rep(c("up", "down"), length.out = 8))  seq_plot() %|%   seq_track(data = seg_gr,             mapping = map(x = start, x_end = x_end,                           y = y, y_end = y_end, color = grp),             windows = win) %+%   seq_segment(aesthetics = aes(linewidth = 2, alpha = 0.85)) -> p p$plot()

what: seq_curve — Bezier curve with data-space control points
title: SeqPlotR Elements: Primitives, Composites, and Ideograms
text: Required: x, y, x_end, y_end. aes(curvature) sets fractional y-offset control points (default 0.3). Handy visualising directional relationships (transcript splice, read pair) without stealing arch slot (seq_arc) used SV calls.
code: curve_gr <- GRanges("chr1",   IRanges(start = c(1.2e6, 1.7e6, 2.3e6), width = 1),   x_end = c(1.5e6, 2.1e6, 2.7e6),   y     = c(0.2,   0.3,   0.25),   y_end = c(0.5,   0.7,   0.6))  seq_plot() %|%   seq_track(data = curve_gr,             mapping = map(x = start, x_end = x_end,                           y = y, y_end = y_end),             windows = win) %+%   seq_curve(aesthetics = aes(curvature = 0.5,                              color = "#66800B",                              linewidth = 1.4)) -> p p$plot()

title: SeqPlotR Elements: Primitives, Composites, and Ideograms
text: Required: x, y. Optional group splits path several disconnected lines. Good plotting per-group traces one track.
code: group_xs <- seq(1.05e6, 2.95e6, length.out = 30) path_gr  <- GRanges("chr1",   IRanges(start = rep(group_xs, 3), width = 1),   y     = c(sin((group_xs - 1e6) / 5e5) * 0.2 + 0.4,             cos((group_xs - 1e6) / 4e5) * 0.2 + 0.5,                  ((group_xs - 1e6) / 2e6) * 0.3 + 0.3),   trace = rep(c("A", "B", "C"), each = 30))  seq_plot() %|%   seq_track(data = path_gr,             mapping = map(x = start, y = y,                           group = trace, color = trace),             windows = win) %+%   seq_path(aesthetics = aes(linewidth = 1.3, alpha = 0.85)) -> p p$plot()

title: SeqPlotR Elements: Primitives, Composites, and Ideograms
text: Required: x, y. Optional group partitions vertices across multiple polygons. Use custom region highlights hand-shaped overlays. : three filled triangles, one per gene body.
code: tri_starts <- c(1.20e6, 1.80e6, 2.40e6) tri_widths <- c(2.5e5,  3.0e5,  2.0e5)  poly_gr <- GRanges("chr1",   IRanges(start = rep(tri_starts, each = 3), width = 1),   x_v = c(rbind(tri_starts,                 tri_starts + tri_widths / 2,                 tri_starts + tri_widths)),   y_v = rep(c(0.2, 0.8, 0.2), times = 3),   grp = rep(paste0("tri", 1:3), each = 3))  seq_plot() %|%   seq_track(data = poly_gr,             mapping = map(x = x_v, y = y_v,                           group = grp, fill = grp),             windows = win) %+%   seq_poly(aesthetics = aes(color = "white", alpha = 0.8)) -> p p$plot()

what: seq_area — filled area under a curve
title: SeqPlotR Elements: Primitives, Composites, and Ideograms
text: Required: x, y. Optional aes(baseline = ...) sets closing y-value (default 0). Coverage curves, pileups, smoothed density fills.
code: area_xs <- seq(1.05e6, 2.95e6, length.out = 80) area_gr <- GRanges("chr1", IRanges(area_xs, width = 1),                    depth = 0.5 + 0.3 * sin((area_xs - 1e6) / 4e5) +                            rnorm(80, 0, 0.02))  seq_plot() %|%   seq_track(data = area_gr,             mapping = map(x = start, y = depth),             windows = win) %+%   seq_area(aesthetics = aes(fill = "#4385BE",                             color = "#205EA6",                             alpha = 0.65,                             linewidth = 0.8)) -> p p$plot()

title: SeqPlotR Elements: Primitives, Composites, and Ideograms
text: Required: x, y, label. Optional: size, color, angle, hjust, vjust.
code: text_gr <- GRanges("chr1",   IRanges(start = c(1.30e6, 1.90e6, 2.50e6), width = 1),   y_pos = c(0.75, 0.45, 0.85),   lbl   = c("peak_1", "peak_2", "peak_3"))  seq_plot() %|%   seq_track(data = text_gr,             mapping = map(x = start, y = y_pos, label = lbl),             windows = win) %+%   seq_text(aesthetics = aes(fontsize = 12,                             color = "#AF3029",                             hjust = 0.5)) -> p p$plot()

title: SeqPlotR Elements: Primitives, Composites, and Ideograms
text: Composites chain primitives (often plus small amount pre-computation) provide higher-level idioms. composite inherits underlying primitive exposes narrower, task-specific map() vocabulary.

title: SeqPlotR Elements: Primitives, Composites, and Ideograms
text: One rectangle per observation; group stacks bars identical x positions.
code: bar_gr <- GRanges("chr1",   IRanges(start = seq(1.1e6, 2.9e6, by = 2e5), width = 8e4),   score = runif(10, 0.2, 1.0))  seq_plot() %|%   seq_track(data = bar_gr,             mapping = map(x = start, y = score),             windows = win) %+%   seq_bar(aesthetics = aes(fill = "#4385BE")) -> p p$plot()

what: seq_ribbon — band between two y series
title: SeqPlotR Elements: Primitives, Composites, and Ideograms
text: Required: y_min, y_max. Confidence bands, min/max pileups, CI envelopes — anything needs shaded interval per x.
code: ribbon_xs <- seq(1.05e6, 2.95e6, length.out = 60) ribbon_mu <- sin((ribbon_xs - 1e6) / 3e5) * 0.3 + 0.5 ribbon_gr <- GRanges("chr1", IRanges(ribbon_xs, width = 1),                      mean = ribbon_mu,                      lo   = ribbon_mu - 0.12,                      hi   = ribbon_mu + 0.12)  seq_plot() %|%   seq_track(data = ribbon_gr,             mapping = map(x = start, y_min = lo, y_max = hi),             windows = win) %+%   seq_ribbon(aesthetics = aes(fill = "#4385BE", alpha = 0.5)) -> p p$plot()

title: SeqPlotR Elements: Primitives, Composites, and Ideograms
text: Calls stats::density() resolved y renders result filled area. Pre-computed densities use seq_area() directly.
code: dens_gr <- GRanges("chr1",   IRanges(start = seq(1e6, 3e6, length.out = 200), width = 1),   score = c(rnorm(120, mean = 0.3, sd = 0.05),             rnorm(80,  mean = 0.7, sd = 0.08)))  seq_plot() %|%   seq_track(data = dens_gr,             mapping = map(x = start, y = score),             windows = win) %+%   seq_density(aesthetics = aes(fill = "#879A39", alpha = 0.7)) -> p p$plot()

title: SeqPlotR Elements: Primitives, Composites, and Ideograms
text: Flat mode: one rectangle per observation. Rotated mode (aes(rotate = TRUE)) turns tile diamond — standard Hi-C representation combined genomic y-axis (see basics vignette full example).
code: tile_gr <- GRanges("chr1",   IRanges(start = seq(1.1e6, 2.9e6, by = 1e5), width = 8e4),   fill_col = sample(flexoki_palette(5), 19, replace = TRUE))  seq_plot() %|%   seq_track(data = tile_gr,             mapping = map(x = start, fill = fill_col),             windows = win) %+%   seq_tile() -> p p$plot()

title: SeqPlotR Elements: Primitives, Composites, and Ideograms
text: Vertical stem baseline (default 0) y, point y. Standard sparse discrete events (mutation impact, peak summits).
code: lp_gr <- GRanges("chr1",   IRanges(start = sample(seq(1.05e6, 2.95e6, by = 1e4), 15), width = 1),   impact = runif(15, 0.2, 1.0))  seq_plot() %|%   seq_track(data = lp_gr,             mapping = map(x = start, y = impact),             windows = win) %+%   seq_lollipop(aesthetics = aes(color = "#AF3029", linewidth = 1.2)) -> p p$plot()

title: SeqPlotR Elements: Primitives, Composites, and Ideograms
text: Every column reference comes map(): group joins features one gene, strand orients arrows, type distinguishes exons UTRs, label places gene name, color tints gene.
code: gene_gr <- GRanges("chr1",   IRanges(     start = c(1.10e6, 1.20e6, 1.35e6, 1.50e6, 1.58e6,               1.90e6, 2.05e6, 2.18e6, 2.30e6,               2.55e6, 2.68e6, 2.80e6),     width = c( 3e4,  6e4,  4e4,  5e4,  2e4,                4e4,  8e4,  5e4,  3e4,                6e4,  3e4,  5e4)   ),   gene_id    = rep(c("A", "B", "C"), times = c(5, 4, 3)),   gene_name  = rep(c("TP53", "MYC", "BRCA1"), times = c(5, 4, 3)),   strand_col = rep(c("+", "-", "+"),       times = c(5, 4, 3)),   feature = c("UTR","exon","exon","exon","UTR",               "UTR","exon","exon","UTR",               "exon","exon","exon"),   color = c(rep("#205EA6", 5),             rep("#AF3029", 4),             rep("#66800B", 3)))  seq_plot() %|%   seq_track(data = gene_gr,             mapping = map(group  = gene_id,                           type   = feature,                           strand = strand_col,                           label  = gene_name,                           color  = color),             windows = win) %+%   seq_gene(map(group  = gene_id,                type   = feature,                strand = strand_col,                label  = gene_name,                color  = color)) -> p p$plot()

title: SeqPlotR Elements: Primitives, Composites, and Ideograms
text: backbone_type controls gene backbone rendering. "arrow" (default) places chevron arrows show strand direction. "solid" draws plain line; "dashed" uses dashed line — useful predicted low-confidence models.
code: seq_plot() %|%   seq_track(data = gene_gr,             mapping = map(group  = gene_id,                           type   = feature,                           strand = strand_col,                           label  = gene_name,                           color  = color),             windows = win) %+%   seq_gene(map(group  = gene_id,                type   = feature,                strand = strand_col,                label  = gene_name,                color  = color),            backbone_type = "dashed") -> p p$plot()

title: SeqPlotR Elements: Primitives, Composites, and Ideograms
text: show_start = TRUE draws flag arrow first exon (row order) gene. genes annotated first exon differs true TSS, pass tss_position = list(gene_id = c(start, end)) override auto-detected position per gene.
code: seq_plot() %|%   seq_track(data = gene_gr,             mapping = map(group  = gene_id,                           type   = feature,                           strand = strand_col,                           label  = gene_name,                           color  = color),             windows = win) %+%   seq_gene(map(group  = gene_id,                type   = feature,                strand = strand_col,                label  = gene_name,                color  = color),            show_start = TRUE) -> p p$plot()

title: SeqPlotR Elements: Primitives, Composites, and Ideograms
text: separate_strands = TRUE partitions track interior two horizontal sub-bands: "+" strand genes top "-" strand genes bottom. dashed line marks band boundary band labelled. Silently ignored one strand present.
code: seq_plot() %|%   seq_track(data = gene_gr,             mapping = map(group  = gene_id,                           type   = feature,                           strand = strand_col,                           label  = gene_name,                           color  = color),             windows = win) %+%   seq_gene(map(group  = gene_id,                type   = feature,                strand = strand_col,                label  = gene_name,                color  = color),            separate_strands = TRUE) -> p p$plot()

title: SeqPlotR Elements: Primitives, Composites, and Ideograms
text: separate_strands, show_start, backbone_type independent compose freely. solid backbone — useful track already conveys directionality strand band layout.
code: seq_plot() %|%   seq_track(data = gene_gr,             mapping = map(group  = gene_id,                           type   = feature,                           strand = strand_col,                           label  = gene_name,                           color  = color),             windows = win,             track_height = 1.2) %+%   seq_gene(map(group  = gene_id,                type   = feature,                strand = strand_col,                label  = gene_name,                color  = color),            backbone_type    = "solid",            show_start       = TRUE,            separate_strands = TRUE) -> p p$plot()

title: SeqPlotR Elements: Primitives, Composites, and Ideograms
text: style_type selects per-gene rendering style. "exon" (default) full backbone-plus-exon-boxes view shown ; "gene" collapses gene single chevron-shaped polygon spanning gene extent — useful exon detail needed screen space tight; "point" reduces gene single filled circle TSS, ideal dense overviews. Labels drawn three modes.
code: seq_plot() %|%   seq_track(data = gene_gr,             mapping = map(group  = gene_id,                           type   = feature,                           strand = strand_col,                           label  = gene_name,                           color  = color),             windows = win) %+%   seq_gene(map(group  = gene_id,                type   = feature,                strand = strand_col,                label  = gene_name,                color  = color),            style_type = "exon") -> p p$plot() seq_plot() %|%   seq_track(data = gene_gr,             mapping = map(group  = gene_id,                           type   = feature,                           strand = strand_col,                           label  = gene_name,                           color  = color),             windows = win) %+%   seq_gene(map(group  = gene_id,                type   = feature,                strand = strand_col,                label  = gene_name,                color  = color),            style_type = "gene") -> p p$plot() seq_plot() %|%   seq_track(data = gene_gr,             mapping = map(group  = gene_id,                           type   = feature,                           strand = strand_col,                           label  = gene_name,                           color  = color),             windows = win) %+%   seq_gene(map(group  = gene_id,                type   = feature,                strand = strand_col,                label  = gene_name,                color  = color),            style_type = "point") -> p p$plot()

title: SeqPlotR Elements: Primitives, Composites, and Ideograms
text: genes overlap, seq_gene stacks non-overlapping tiers automatically. separate_strands = TRUE strand band tier stack, keeping two strands visually separated even high gene density.
code: set.seed(7) n_genes  <- 14 starts   <- sort(sample(seq(1.05e6, 2.6e6, by = 5e4), n_genes)) widths   <- sample(seq(5e4, 3e5, by = 2e4), n_genes, replace = TRUE) strands  <- sample(c("+", "-"), n_genes, replace = TRUE) colors_d <- ifelse(strands == "+", "#205EA6", "#AF3029") gids_d   <- paste0("G", seq_len(n_genes))  dense_gr <- GRanges("chr1",   IRanges(start = starts, width = widths),   gene_id    = gids_d,   gene_name  = gids_d,   strand_col = strands,   feature    = "exon",   color      = colors_d)  seq_plot() %|%   seq_track(data = dense_gr,             mapping = map(group  = gene_id,                           type   = feature,                           strand = strand_col,                           label  = gene_name,                           color  = color),             windows = win,             track_height = 1.4) %+%   seq_gene(map(group  = gene_id,                type   = feature,                strand = strand_col,                label  = gene_name,                color  = color),            separate_strands = TRUE) -> p p$plot()

title: SeqPlotR Elements: Primitives, Composites, and Ideograms
text: seq_sequence() renders coloured rectangle nucleotide windows 200 bp wide. Wider windows emit message render nothing. Colours follow UCSC standard default: green, T red, C blue, G orange/gold. rectangle drawn 99 % slot width thin gap separates adjacent bases. Supply nucleotides via sequence (character string) fetched automatically BSgenome package via genome. Set show_letters = TRUE overlay base letter windows ≤ 80 bp.
code: seq50  <- paste(rep(c("A","T","C","G","G","A","C","T","T","A"), 5), collapse = "") win50  <- GRanges("chr1", IRanges(1, 50))  seq_plot() %|%   seq_track(windows = win50, track_height = 0.7) %+%   seq_sequence(sequence = seq50, show_letters = TRUE) -> p p$plot()

title: SeqPlotR Elements: Primitives, Composites, and Ideograms
text: window 80 200 bp letters suppressed (show_letters silently ignored) coloured blocks still render. gives compact composition pattern view without clutter overlapping labels.
code: seq100 <- paste(rep(c("A","T","C","G"), 25), collapse = "") win100 <- GRanges("chr1", IRanges(1, 100))  seq_plot() %|%   seq_track(windows = win100, track_height = 0.7) %+%   seq_sequence(sequence = seq100) -> p p$plot()

title: SeqPlotR Elements: Primitives, Composites, and Ideograms
text: Pass named character vector colors override UCSC defaults. , soft pastel palette used make track less visually dominant layered elements.
code: pastel <- c(A = "#A8D8A8", T = "#F4A7A7", C = "#A7C4F4", G = "#FAD9A1",             N = "#DDDDDD")  seq_plot() %|%   seq_track(windows = win50, track_height = 0.7) %+%   seq_sequence(sequence = seq50, show_letters = TRUE, colors = pastel) -> p p$plot()

title: SeqPlotR Elements: Primitives, Composites, and Ideograms
text: rect_height reserves fraction track height rectangles — useful placing seq_sequence signal track. sequence track gets short track_height allocation coverage area dominates.
code: win60  <- GRanges("chr1", IRanges(1, 60)) seq60  <- paste(rep(c("A","C","G","T"), 15), collapse = "")  cov_xs60 <- seq(1, 60, length.out = 60) cov_gr60 <- GRanges("chr1", IRanges(start = cov_xs60, width = 1),                     depth = 0.4 + 0.4 * sin(seq(0, 2 * pi, length.out = 60)))  seq_plot() %|%   seq_track(windows = win60, track_height = 0.55) %+%     seq_sequence(sequence = seq60,                  show_letters = TRUE,                  rect_height  = 0.7) %__%   seq_track(data = cov_gr60,             mapping = map(x = start, y = depth),             windows = win60) %+%     seq_area(aesthetics = aes(fill = "#4385BE",                               color = "#205EA6",                               alpha = 0.65,                               linewidth = 0.8)) -> p p$plot()

title: SeqPlotR Elements: Primitives, Composites, and Ideograms
text: seq_ideogram() consumes GRanges cytogenetic bands gieStain mcol draws band Giemsa-shaded rectangle. Paired acen bands collapse two red triangles meeting centromere. Load bundled hg38 cytoband table [load_cytobands()].

title: SeqPlotR Elements: Primitives, Composites, and Ideograms
text: create_genome_windows("chr1") expands GRanges spanning full chromosome, ideogram fills panel end--end.
code: cb <- load_cytobands()  seq_plot() %|%   seq_track(track_id = "chr1",             windows = create_genome_windows("chr1")) %+%   seq_ideogram(data = cb) -> p p$plot()

title: SeqPlotR Elements: Primitives, Composites, and Ideograms
text: windows GRanges multiple ranges lays chromosome panel inside track. handful chromosomes stacked side--side gives --glance karyotype.
code: karyo_win <- create_genome_windows(c("chr1", "chr4", "chr7",                                      "chr14", "chrX"))  seq_plot() %|%   seq_track(track_id = "Karyotype",             windows = karyo_win) %+%   seq_ideogram(data = cb) -> p p$plot()

title: SeqPlotR Elements: Primitives, Composites, and Ideograms
text: multi-range windows GRanges lays every range separate panel inside single track. Mappings resolve per-panel, element draws independently region. : two non-adjacent regions chr1 rendered side--side panels.  Windows can carry per-panel scale mcols bias relative widths — e.g. 1 Mb region next 100 kb region shown panel width smaller region legible:
code: starts_A <- seq(1.1e6, 2.9e6, length.out = 12) starts_B <- seq(5.1e6, 6.4e6, length.out = 12)  multi_gr <- GRanges("chr1",   IRanges(start = c(starts_A, starts_B), width = 5e4),   score  = runif(24, 0.2, 1.0),   region = rep(c("A", "B"), each = 12))  seq_plot() %|%   seq_track(data = multi_gr,             mapping = map(x = start, y = score, group = region),             windows = multi_win) %+%   seq_bar() -> p p$plot() scaled_win <- GRanges("chr1",   IRanges(start = c(1.0e6, 5.0e6), end = c(3.0e6, 5.1e6))) mcols(scaled_win)$scale <- c(1e-6, 1e-5)  starts_C <- seq(1.1e6, 2.9e6, length.out = 12) starts_D <- seq(5.01e6, 5.09e6, length.out = 12) scaled_gr <- GRanges("chr1",   IRanges(start = c(starts_C, starts_D), width = 2e4),   score  = runif(24, 0.2, 1.0))  seq_plot() %|%   seq_track(data = scaled_gr,             mapping = map(x = start, y = score),             windows = scaled_win) %+%   seq_lollipop(aesthetics = aes(color = "#AF3029")) -> p p$plot()

title: SeqPlotR Elements: Primitives, Composites, and Ideograms
text: seq_plot(layout = "...") accepts patchwork string non-# letter track_id-addressed cell. example lays six-track genome browser two regions: ideogram strip across top, signal ribbon + density sidebar next row, bar track lollipop track middle, gene models spanning full width bottom row. seq_preview_layout() shows geometry -front — data required — can iterate string independently element code.  track’s data / mapping declared independently. Tracks whose track_id isn’t present layout string silently skipped, operator chain can reused across different layouts swapping string.  set elements assembles positional chain — useful track count modest patchwork string overkill. 2-row, 2-region browser pairs coverage area multi-region bar track gene strip:  track’s panels align vertically across rows share windows GRanges — left panel Coverage, Bars, Genes show 1–3 Mb region, right panel shows 5–6.5 Mb. Cross-track links (seq_string, seq_synteny, seq_zoom) can dropped top layout like ; see links vignette worked examples.
code: layout_str <- " IIII AAAB CCDD GGGG GGGG " seq_preview_layout(layout = layout_str) # Shared elements + per-track datasets -------------------------------------- sig_xs <- seq(1.05e6, 2.95e6, length.out = 80) sig_mu <- sin((sig_xs - 1e6) / 3e5) * 0.25 + 0.55 sig_gr <- GRanges("chr1", IRanges(sig_xs, width = 1),                   mean = sig_mu,                   lo   = sig_mu - 0.1,                   hi   = sig_mu + 0.1)  density_gr <- GRanges("chr1",   IRanges(start = seq(1e6, 3e6, length.out = 200), width = 1),   score = c(rnorm(120, 0.3, 0.05),             rnorm(80,  0.7, 0.08)))  mut_gr <- GRanges("chr1",   IRanges(start = sample(seq(1.05e6, 2.95e6, by = 1e4), 18), width = 1),   impact = runif(18, 0.2, 1.0))  seq_plot(layout = layout_str) %+%   # I — ideogram strip   seq_track(track_id = "I",             windows = create_genome_windows("chr1:1000000-3000000")) %+%     seq_ideogram(data = cb) %+%   # A — signal ribbon   seq_track(track_id = "A",             data = sig_gr,             mapping = map(x = start, y_min = lo, y_max = hi),             windows = win) %+%     seq_ribbon(aesthetics = aes(fill = "#4385BE", alpha = 0.5)) %+%   # B — density sidebar   seq_track(track_id = "B",             data = density_gr,             mapping = map(x = start, y = score),             windows = win) %+%     seq_density(aesthetics = aes(fill = "#879A39", alpha = 0.7)) %+%   # C — bar summary   seq_track(track_id = "C",             data = bar_gr,             mapping = map(x = start, y = score),             windows = win) %+%     seq_bar(aesthetics = aes(fill = "#8B7EC8")) %+%   # D — lollipop mutation calls   seq_track(track_id = "D",             data = mut_gr,             mapping = map(x = start, y = impact),             windows = win) %+%     seq_lollipop(aesthetics = aes(color = "#AF3029", linewidth = 1.2)) %+%   # G — gene models spanning the full bottom row   seq_track(track_id = "G",             data = gene_gr,             mapping = map(group  = gene_id,                           type   = feature,                           strand = strand_col,                           label  = gene_name,                           color  = color),             windows = win) %+%     seq_gene(map(group  = gene_id,                  type   = feature,                  strand = strand_col,                  label  = gene_name,                  color  = color)) -> p p$plot() # Coverage signal spanning both regions multi_sig_xs <- c(seq(1.05e6, 2.95e6, length.out = 60),                   seq(5.05e6, 6.45e6, length.out = 50)) multi_sig_gr <- GRanges("chr1", IRanges(multi_sig_xs, width = 1),   depth = 0.5 + 0.25 * sin((multi_sig_xs - 1e6) / 4e5))  # Gene models split across the two regions multi_gene_gr <- GRanges("chr1",   IRanges(start = c(1.20e6, 1.35e6, 1.50e6,                     2.05e6, 2.18e6, 2.30e6,                     5.15e6, 5.25e6, 5.38e6,                     6.10e6, 6.20e6, 6.32e6),           width = rep(c(8e4, 6e4, 5e4), 4)),   gene_id    = rep(c("G1", "G2", "G3", "G4"), each = 3),   gene_name  = rep(c("G1", "G2", "G3", "G4"), each = 3),   strand_col = rep(c("+", "-", "+", "-"),     each = 3),   feature = rep("exon", 12),   color   = rep(c("#205EA6", "#AF3029", "#66800B", "#BC5215"), each = 3))  seq_plot() %|%   seq_track(track_id = "Coverage",             data = multi_sig_gr,             mapping = map(x = start, y = depth),             windows = multi_win, track_height = 1.0) %+%     seq_area(aesthetics = aes(fill = "#4385BE",                               color = "#205EA6",                               alpha = 0.55,                               linewidth = 0.7)) %__%   seq_track(track_id = "Bars",             data = multi_gr,             mapping = map(x = start, y = score, group = region),             windows = multi_win, track_height = 0.8) %+%     seq_bar() %__%   seq_track(track_id = "Genes",             data = multi_gene_gr,             mapping = map(group  = gene_id,                           type   = feature,                           strand = strand_col,                           label  = gene_name,                           color  = color),             windows = multi_win, track_height = 1.2) %+%     seq_gene(map(group  = gene_id,                  type   = feature,                  strand = strand_col,                  label  = gene_name,                  color  = color)) -> p p$plot()

title: Hi-C Visualization
text: examples use Hi-C contact simulator carried THEfunc/inst/examples/08_rotated_hic_heatmap.R: every upper-triangular bin pair (, j) assigned contact strength exp(-distance * decay_rate) * lognormal(noise), symmetrised mirroring (j, ).
code: # Hi-C simulator (THEfunc port). generate_hic_matrix <- function(n_bins, decay_rate = 0.2) {   ij    <- expand.grid(i = seq_len(n_bins), j = seq_len(n_bins))   upper <- ij$j >= ij$i   ij    <- ij[upper, , drop = FALSE]   d     <- ij$j - ij$i   s     <- pmax(0.01, exp(-d * decay_rate) * rlnorm(nrow(ij), 0, 0.3))   upper_df <- data.frame(bin_i = ij$i, bin_j = ij$j, strength = s)   off      <- d > 0   lower_df <- data.frame(bin_i = ij$j[off], bin_j = ij$i[off],                          strength = s[off])   rbind(upper_df, lower_df) }  # Convenience: build a `seq_hic`-shaped GRanges for a single region. hic_region <- function(chrom, start, end, bin_size, decay = 0.2,                        seed = NULL) {   if (!is.null(seed)) set.seed(seed)   bin_starts <- seq(start, end - bin_size, by = bin_size)   n          <- length(bin_starts)   mat        <- generate_hic_matrix(n, decay_rate = decay)   GRanges(chrom,           IRanges(bin_starts[mat$bin_i], width = bin_size),           i_start = bin_starts[mat$bin_i],           i_end   = bin_starts[mat$bin_i] + bin_size,           j_start = bin_starts[mat$bin_j],           j_end   = bin_starts[mat$bin_j] + bin_size,           score   = mat$strength) }  # Multi-region: per-chromosome intra-chrom matrices, concatenated. hic_multi <- function(regions, bin_size, decay = 0.2, base_seed = 100) {   parts <- lapply(seq_along(regions), function(k) {     r <- regions[[k]]     hic_region(r[[1]], r[[2]], r[[3]], bin_size,                decay = decay, seed = base_seed + k)   })   do.call(c, parts) }

what: Multiple regions on the y axis
title: Hi-C Visualization
text: y_windows controls genomic y-axis full diagonal styles independently windows. default mirrors windows (square matrix). Pass multi-region GRanges stack several y-axis windows vertically — gets sub-panel axis labels title naming chromosome range, thin horizontal separator marks boundary. represent inter-chromosomal contacts, input GRanges carries optional j_chrom mcols column giving j-bin’s chromosome (GRanges seqname -bin’s chromosome). j_chrom absent bins taken chromosome.  bottom panel shows chr1 × chrA contacts; top panel shows chr1 × chrB. y sub-panel genomic scale, two pairs directly comparable.
code: # x: chr1 (1 region). y: stacked chrA + chrB (2 regions). # Mixed contact set: chr1×chr1 (intra), chr1↔chrA, chr1↔chrB. set.seed(7) n_per <- 250 i_st  <- sort(sample(seq(1, 4e6 - 1e5, by = 1e5), n_per, replace = TRUE)) mk <- function(j_chrom) data.frame(   i_chrom = "chr1",   i_start = i_st, i_end = i_st + 1e5,   j_chrom = j_chrom,   j_start = sort(sample(seq(1, 4e6 - 1e5, by = 1e5), n_per, replace = TRUE)),   score   = rexp(n_per, rate = 0.4),   stringsAsFactors = FALSE ) df <- rbind(mk("chrA"), mk("chrB")) df$j_end <- df$j_start + 1e5  gr_multiy <- GRanges(   df$i_chrom, IRanges(df$i_start, df$i_end),   i_start = df$i_start, i_end = df$i_end,   j_chrom = df$j_chrom, j_start = df$j_start, j_end = df$j_end,   score   = df$score )  win_x_one <- GRanges("chr1",            IRanges(1, 4e6)) win_y_two <- GRanges(c("chrA", "chrB"), IRanges(c(1, 1), c(4e6, 4e6))) seq_hic(gr_multiy,         windows   = win_x_one,         y_windows = win_y_two,         style     = "full")$plot()

what: Combining x-axis regions into a single panel
title: Hi-C Visualization
text: default x-axis region windows renders panel. Setting combine_windows = TRUE concatenates single virtual panel — useful cross-window contacts (e.g. inter-chromosomal translocation) need appear one continuous view. original window keeps per-window axis labels title, thin vertical separator marks boundary. combine_windows implemented [seq_track()] level, option available every track type — just Hi-C.
code: # Two-region data: intra-chr14, intra-chr5, plus a "translocation" # band of inter-chromosomal contacts concentrated near a breakpoint. set.seed(11) bs   <- 1e5  # bin size mk_intra <- function(chrom, start, end, decay = 0.25, seed_off = 0) {   starts <- seq(start, end - bs, by = bs)   n      <- length(starts)   set.seed(seed_off)   mat <- generate_hic_matrix(n, decay_rate = decay)   data.frame(i_chrom = chrom,  i_start = starts[mat$bin_i],              i_end   = starts[mat$bin_i] + bs,              j_chrom = chrom,  j_start = starts[mat$bin_j],              j_end   = starts[mat$bin_j] + bs,              score   = mat$strength,              stringsAsFactors = FALSE) } mk_inter <- function(c1, s1, e1, c2, s2, e2, n = 200, focus = c(0.4, 0.7)) {   i_st <- s1 + round(runif(n, focus[1], focus[2]) * (e1 - s1) / bs) * bs   j_st <- s2 + round(runif(n, focus[1], focus[2]) * (e2 - s2) / bs) * bs   rbind(     data.frame(i_chrom = c1, i_start = i_st, i_end = i_st + bs,                j_chrom = c2, j_start = j_st, j_end = j_st + bs,                score   = rexp(n, rate = 0.4) + 0.5,                stringsAsFactors = FALSE),     data.frame(i_chrom = c2, i_start = j_st, i_end = j_st + bs,                j_chrom = c1, j_start = i_st, j_end = i_st + bs,                score   = rexp(n, rate = 0.4) + 0.5,                stringsAsFactors = FALSE)   ) }  intra14 <- mk_intra("chr14", 98e6,  100e6, seed_off = 1) intra5  <- mk_intra("chr5",  170e6, 172e6, seed_off = 2) inter   <- mk_inter("chr14", 98e6,  100e6, "chr5", 170e6, 172e6, n = 250) all_df  <- rbind(intra14, intra5, inter)  gr_combined <- GRanges(   all_df$i_chrom, IRanges(all_df$i_start, all_df$i_end),   i_start = all_df$i_start, i_end = all_df$i_end,   j_chrom = all_df$j_chrom, j_start = all_df$j_start, j_end = all_df$j_end,   score   = all_df$score ) win_combined <- GRanges(c("chr14", "chr5"),                         IRanges(c(98e6, 170e6), c(100e6, 172e6)))

what: Multiple regions on both axes — composing a region grid
title: Hi-C Visualization
text: 2-D matrix--matrices view (every x-region paired every y-region), build one seq_hic() per cell lay patchwork string. sidesteps need multi-region y sub-axes inside single track gives full control per-cell sizing labels.  diagonal cells (top-left, bottom-right) intra-chromosomal contact maps; -diagonals show asymmetric chrA × chrB chrB × chrA views.
code: # Two-region intra-chrom data plus a contrived "off-diagonal" view. gr_A <- hic_region("chrA", 0, 3e6, bin_size = 1e5, decay = 0.20, seed = 11) gr_B <- hic_region("chrB", 0, 3e6, bin_size = 1e5, decay = 0.30, seed = 12) win_A <- GRanges("chrA", IRanges(1, 3e6)) win_B <- GRanges("chrB", IRanges(1, 3e6)) layout <- " AB CD " p_AA <- seq_hic(gr_A, windows = win_A, style = "full",                 track_id = "A") p_AB <- seq_hic(gr_A, windows = win_A, y_windows = win_B,                 style = "full", track_id = "B") p_BA <- seq_hic(gr_B, windows = win_B, y_windows = win_A,                 style = "full", track_id = "C") p_BB <- seq_hic(gr_B, windows = win_B, style = "full",                 track_id = "D")  fig <- seq_plot(layout = layout) fig <- seq_resolve(fig, p_AA, p_AB, p_BA, p_BB) fig$plot()
