title: Hi-C Visualization
text: seq_hic() one call per style — seq_resolve() makes easy combine several Hi-C views single figure. Useful one style answers one question (long-range patterns via triangle) another different one (TAD structure via full).
code: gr <- hic_region("chr1", 40e6, 45e6, bin_size = 1e5, decay = 0.25,                  seed = 1) win <- GRanges("chr1", IRanges(40e6, 45e6))  p_full <- seq_hic(gr, windows = win, style = "full",                   track_id = "FullView") p_tri  <- seq_hic(gr, windows = win, style = "triangle",                   track_id = "TriView")  fig <- seq_resolve(seq_plot(), p_full, p_tri) fig$plot()

title: Hi-C Visualization
text: Rotated styles (triangle, rectangle) compose naturally annotation tracks share x-axis. seq_resolve() operator chain stitches together:
code: # Synthetic gene track for the same window. genes <- GRanges("chr1",                  IRanges(start = c(40.5e6, 41.4e6, 42.6e6, 43.5e6, 44.2e6),                          width = c(1.2e5, 2.0e5, 8e4,    1.5e5, 9e4)),                  gene = paste0("g", 1:5),                  type = "exon",                  strand = c("+", "-", "+", "+", "-"))  p_hic <- seq_hic(gr, windows = win, style = "triangle",                  track_id = "HiC")  p_genes <- seq_plot() %+%   seq_track(data = genes, windows = win, track_id = "Genes",             track_height = 0.4) %+%   seq_gene(map(group = gene, type = type, strand = strand,                label = gene))  seq_resolve(seq_plot(), p_hic, p_genes)$plot()

title: Legends in SeqPlotR
code: library(SeqPlotR) #>  #> Attaching package: 'SeqPlotR' #> The following object is masked from 'package:base': #>  #>     %||% library(GenomicRanges) #> Loading required package: stats4 #> Loading required package: BiocGenerics #> Loading required package: generics #>  #> Attaching package: 'generics' #> The following objects are masked from 'package:base': #>  #>     as.difftime, as.factor, as.ordered, intersect, is.element, setdiff, #>     setequal, union #>  #> Attaching package: 'BiocGenerics' #> The following objects are masked from 'package:stats': #>  #>     IQR, mad, sd, var, xtabs #> The following objects are masked from 'package:base': #>  #>     anyDuplicated, aperm, append, as.data.frame, basename, cbind, #>     colnames, dirname, do.call, duplicated, eval, evalq, Filter, Find, #>     get, grep, grepl, is.unsorted, lapply, Map, mapply, match, mget, #>     order, paste, pmax, pmax.int, pmin, pmin.int, Position, rank, #>     rbind, Reduce, rownames, sapply, saveRDS, table, tapply, unique, #>     unsplit, which.max, which.min #> Loading required package: S4Vectors #>  #> Attaching package: 'S4Vectors' #> The following object is masked from 'package:utils': #>  #>     findMatches #> The following objects are masked from 'package:base': #>  #>     expand.grid, I, unname #> Loading required package: IRanges #> Loading required package: Seqinfo  # 1-Mb window; axis labels will read 0.0 – 1.0 (scaled by 1e-6) win <- GRanges("chr1", IRanges(1, 1e6))  # Synthetic ChIP-seq signal: sparse background with 8 sharp peaks make_chip <- function(n = 200, n_peaks = 8, seed = 1) {   set.seed(seed)   starts <- sort(sample(1:999000, n))   scores <- runif(n, 0, 0.15)                        # flat baseline   peaks  <- sort(sample(seq_len(n), n_peaks))   for (pk in peaks) {     w <- max(1, pk - 3):min(n, pk + 3)     scores[w] <- scores[w] + runif(length(w), 0.4, 0.85)   }   GRanges("chr1", IRanges(starts, width = 1), score = pmin(scores, 1)) }  gr_a <- make_chip(seed = 1)   # H3K27ac-like gr_b <- make_chip(seed = 2)   # H3K4me3-like  # Continuous-score data for gradient legend examples set.seed(42) n_pts  <- 120 gr_cnt <- GRanges(   "chr1",   IRanges(sort(sample(1:999000, n_pts)), width = 1),   score = round(runif(n_pts, 0, 100), 1) )

title: Legends in SeqPlotR
text: Inside legends overlaid directly data panel. require extra margin space.

what: Quick placement with a bare LegendKey
title: Legends in SeqPlotR
text: Pass bare LegendKey legend automatically wrapped inside legend centered panel (x = 0.5, y = 0.5). seq_legend() call needed: bare LegendKey auto-placed panel centre.
code: p1 <- seq_plot() %+%   seq_track(data = gr_a, mapping = map(x = start, y = score),             windows = win) %+%   seq_area(     aesthetics = aes(fill = "#D08080", alpha = 0.8),     legend     = LegendKey(label = "H3K27ac",                            color = "#A02020", fill = "#D08080", shape = "-")   ) p1$plot()

title: Legends in SeqPlotR
text: Use seq_legend() pin legend specific corner. x y fractions panel width height; y = 0.95 places anchor near top: seq_legend() anchors legend top-left corner title.
code: k1 <- LegendKey(label = "H3K27ac", color = "#A02020", fill = "#D08080", shape = "-") k2 <- LegendKey(label = "H3K4me3", color = "#205080", fill = "#8090C0", shape = "-")  spec_inside <- seq_legend(   list(k1, k2),   title       = "ChIP marks",   position    = "inside",   x           = 0.02,   # 2% from the left edge   y           = 0.95,   # 95% of the way up the panel   orientation = "vertical" )  p2 <- seq_plot() %+%   seq_track(data = gr_a, mapping = map(x = start, y = score),             windows = win) %+%   seq_area(     aesthetics = aes(fill = "#D08080", alpha = 0.8),     legend     = spec_inside   ) p2$plot()

title: Legends in SeqPlotR
text: hjust = 1 right-aligns key block; orientation = "horizontal" puts keys one row: Two keys horizontal row, right-aligned top.
code: spec_h <- seq_legend(   list(k1, k2),   title       = "ChIP marks",   position    = "inside",   x           = 0.98, hjust = 1,   # right-align   y           = 0.95,   orientation = "horizontal" )  p3 <- seq_plot() %+%   seq_track(data = gr_a, mapping = map(x = start, y = score),             windows = win) %+%   seq_area(     aesthetics = aes(fill = "#D08080", alpha = 0.8),     legend     = spec_h   ) p3$plot()

title: Legends in SeqPlotR
text: "track_margin" draws legend canvas margin beside one specific track. Set side choose edge enlarge matching plot margin room render: Two-key vertical legend 18% left canvas margin beside track. orientation defaults "vertical" side %% c("left", "right") "horizontal" top / bottom:
code: spec_tm <- seq_legend(   list(k1, k2),   title       = "ChIP marks",   position    = "track_margin",   side        = "left",   orientation = "vertical",   x           = 0.1,    # fraction of margin width   y           = 0.8     # fraction of margin height (0 = bottom, 1 = top) )  p_tm <- seq_plot(   aesthetics = aes(margins = list(top   = 0.03, bottom = 0.03,                                    left  = 0.18, right  = 0.03)) ) %+%   seq_track(data = gr_a, mapping = map(x = start, y = score),             windows = win) %+%   seq_area(     aesthetics = aes(fill = "#D08080", alpha = 0.8),     legend     = spec_tm   ) p_tm$plot() seq_legend(k1, position = "track_margin", side = "left")$orientation #> [1] "vertical" seq_legend(k1, position = "track_margin", side = "top")$orientation #> [1] "horizontal"

title: Legends in SeqPlotR
text: "canvas_margin" collects legend entries tracks renders , spanning full width chosen margin side. Keys specs share title merged one heading. Use %__% instead %+% stack second track first (direction = ""). Without stacking tracks sit side--side single row: Two stacked tracks sharing single canvas-margin legend top. track titles differ, canvas_margin inserts separator groups. two tracks use different titles show grouping: Two title groups separated canvas margin.
code: # Each spec carries the key for one track. # The first spec's x / hjust controls alignment of the aggregated block. spec_ca <- seq_legend(k1, title = "ChIP marks", position = "canvas_margin",                       side = "top", x = 0.5, hjust = 0.5) spec_cb <- seq_legend(k2, title = "ChIP marks", position = "canvas_margin",                       side = "top")  p_cm <- seq_plot(   aesthetics = aes(margins = list(top   = 0.12, bottom = 0.04,                                    left  = 0.04, right  = 0.04)) ) %+%   seq_track(data = gr_a, mapping = map(x = start, y = score),             windows = win) %+%   seq_area(aesthetics = aes(fill = "#D08080", alpha = 0.8), legend = spec_ca) %__%   seq_track(data = gr_b, mapping = map(x = start, y = score),             windows = win) %+%   seq_area(aesthetics = aes(fill = "#8090C0", alpha = 0.8), legend = spec_cb)  p_cm$plot() spec_ca2 <- seq_legend(k1, title = "Active enhancers",                        position = "canvas_margin", side = "top",                        x = 0.5, hjust = 0.5) spec_cb2 <- seq_legend(k2, title = "Promoters",                        position = "canvas_margin", side = "top")  p_cm2 <- seq_plot(   aesthetics = aes(margins = list(top   = 0.14, bottom = 0.04,                                    left  = 0.04, right  = 0.04)) ) %+%   seq_track(data = gr_a, mapping = map(x = start, y = score),             windows = win) %+%   seq_area(aesthetics = aes(fill = "#D08080", alpha = 0.8), legend = spec_ca2) %__%   seq_track(data = gr_b, mapping = map(x = start, y = score),             windows = win) %+%   seq_area(aesthetics = aes(fill = "#8090C0", alpha = 0.8), legend = spec_cb2)  p_cm2$plot()

title: Legends in SeqPlotR
text: Continuous color scales need color bar rather discrete key rows. seq_gradient_legend() produces GradientLegendSpec renders filled rectangle graded palette’s low high color, tick-- label axis derived data limits. placement arguments (position, x, y, hjust, orientation, side) work identically seq_legend().

what: Manual color bar inside the panel
title: Legends in SeqPlotR
text: Vertical viridis color bar pinned right side panel.
code: grad_inside <- seq_gradient_legend(   palette     = "viridis",   limits      = c(0, 100),   title       = "Score",   position    = "inside",   x           = 0.97,          # near the right edge   y           = 0.5,           # vertically centred   hjust       = 1,   orientation = "vertical" )  p_gi <- seq_plot() %+%   seq_track(data = gr_cnt, mapping = map(x = start, y = score),             windows = win) %+%   seq_point(     aesthetics = aes(color = "#2166AC", size = 1.2),     legend     = grad_inside   ) p_gi$plot()

what: Color bar in the track margin
title: Legends in SeqPlotR
text: Place bar right track margin clean separation data: Horizontal plasma color bar right track margin.
code: grad_tm <- seq_gradient_legend(   palette     = "plasma",   limits      = c(0, 100),   title       = "Score",   position    = "track_margin",   side        = "right",   orientation = "vertical",   x           = 0.5,   y           = 0.5 )  p_gtm <- seq_plot(   aesthetics = aes(margins = list(top    = 0.03, bottom = 0.03,                                    left   = 0.03, right  = 0.20)) ) %+%   seq_track(data = gr_cnt, mapping = map(x = start, y = score),             windows = win) %+%   seq_point(     aesthetics = aes(color = "#2166AC", size = 1.2),     legend     = grad_tm   ) p_gtm$plot()

title: Legends in SeqPlotR
text: continuous column mapped via map() supported element (SeqBar, SeqLine, SeqArea, SeqPoint, SeqSegment, SeqTile, SeqRect), GradientLegendSpec generated automatically — seq_gradient_legend() call needed. bar defaults position = "track_margin", side = "right". Pass show_legend = FALSE opt specific element: Auto color bar map(fill = score); seq_gradient_legend() needed.
code: p_auto <- seq_plot(   aesthetics = aes(margins = list(top    = 0.03, bottom = 0.03,                                    left   = 0.03, right  = 0.20)) ) %+%   seq_track(data = gr_cnt, mapping = map(x = start, y = score),             windows = win) %+%   seq_bar(     mapping    = map(fill = score),     aesthetics = aes(alpha = 0.9)     # show_legend = FALSE   # uncomment to suppress the auto bar   ) p_auto$plot()

title: Legends in SeqPlotR
text: breaks accepts three forms: NULL (default) — pretty() five ticks. single integer — pretty() targeting many ticks. numeric vector — exact break values. Color bar explicit breaks 0, 25, 50, 75, 100.
code: grad_breaks <- seq_gradient_legend(   palette     = "magma",   limits      = c(0, 100),   breaks      = c(0, 25, 50, 75, 100),   title       = "Score",   position    = "inside",   x           = 0.97,   y           = 0.5,   hjust       = 1,   orientation = "vertical" )  p_brk <- seq_plot() %+%   seq_track(data = gr_cnt, mapping = map(x = start, y = score),             windows = win) %+%   seq_point(     aesthetics = aes(color = "#8B3A8B", size = 1.2),     legend     = grad_breaks   ) p_brk$plot()

title: Legends in SeqPlotR
text: Legends can silenced three granularities. level overrides finer-grained settings .

title: Legends in SeqPlotR
text: show_legend = FALSE one element silences element, leaving others track unaffected: also suppresses auto-generated gradient bars:
code: el_shown  <- seq_area(legend = LegendKey(label = "Foreground")) el_hidden <- seq_area(legend = LegendKey(label = "Background"),                       show_legend = FALSE)  is.null(el_shown$collect_legend_keys())   # FALSE — contributes #> [1] FALSE is.null(el_hidden$collect_legend_keys())  # TRUE  — suppressed #> [1] TRUE el_no_bar <- seq_bar(mapping = map(fill = score), show_legend = FALSE) is.null(el_no_bar$auto_legend)   # TRUE — no bar generated #> [1] TRUE

title: Legends in SeqPlotR
text: show_legend = FALSE seq_track() silences every element track, regardless individual element settings:
code: trk <- seq_track(windows = win,                  elements  = list(el_shown, el_hidden),                  show_legend = FALSE) is.null(trk$collect_legend_keys())   # TRUE — whole track suppressed #> [1] TRUE

title: Legends in SeqPlotR
text: Pass show_legend = FALSE (shorthand legend = FALSE) seq_plot() suppress legend output entire plot, including auto-generated gradient bars: legend = FALSE seq_plot() removes legend output.
code: p_no_legend <- seq_plot(legend = FALSE) %+%   seq_track(data = gr_a, mapping = map(x = start, y = score),             windows = win) %+%   seq_area(     aesthetics = aes(fill = "#D08080", alpha = 0.8),     legend     = spec_inside   # spec defined earlier — ignored here   ) p_no_legend$plot()

title: Legends in SeqPlotR
text: collect_legend_keys() aggregates legend entries element, track, (transitively) tracks plot. returns NULL nothing contributes, list named lists: Track-level aggregation flattens element entries:
code: k  <- LegendKey(label = "ChIP signal", title = "Tracks", color = "darkgreen") el <- seq_area(legend = k) str(el$collect_legend_keys()[[1]]) #> List of 3 #>  $ title        : chr "Tracks" #>  $ key          :List of 9 #>   ..$ label: chr "ChIP signal" #>   ..$ title: chr "Tracks" #>   ..$ color: chr "darkgreen" #>   ..$ shape: chr "-" #>   ..$ size : num 1 #>   ..$ alpha: num 1 #>   ..$ fill : NULL #>   ..$ lty  : num 1 #>   ..$ extra: list() #>   ..- attr(*, "class")= chr "LegendKey" #>  $ element_class: chr "SeqArea" el1 <- seq_area(legend = LegendKey(label = "H3K27ac", color = "#A02020")) el2 <- seq_area(legend = LegendKey(label = "H3K4me3", color = "#205080"))  trk  <- seq_track(windows = win, elements = list(el1, el2)) keys <- trk$collect_legend_keys() cat(length(keys), "keys from track:\n") #> 2 keys from track: for (entry in keys)   cat(" ", entry$key$label, "—", entry$element_class, "\n") #>   H3K27ac — SeqArea  #>   H3K4me3 — SeqArea

title: SeqPlotR Links: Arches, SV Reconstruction, and Cross-Track Connectors
text: Every link two anchors. SeqPlotR encodes single data argument paired column names — data2/mapping2. data may : data.frame (BEDPE-like). anchor fields come named columns. GRanges whose mcols carry anchor-1 fields. GRanges contributes anchor 0 via seqnames / start / strand; anchor 1 read mcols columns named map(). plain GRanges (single locus). Anchor 0 start, anchor 1 end range — used compact within-locus arcs. map() vocabulary always explicit: GRanges accessors seqnames strand injected map() specials alongside start / end / width / mid, typical GRanges--mcols call can use bare names: map(chrom0 = seqnames, strand0 = strand, ...).

title: SeqPlotR Links: Arches, SV Reconstruction, and Cross-Track Connectors
text: Inside seq_track (added via %+%): t0 t1 locked track. Use within-track arcs summarise intra-region relationships. seq_plot level (added via %+%): t0 t1 unset, link attaches recently added track (just like element). t0 / t1 set, link deferred — stored plot drawn last, every track laid .

title: SeqPlotR Links: Arches, SV Reconstruction, and Cross-Track Connectors
text: seq_arc draws single Bezier arch two loci. simplest form encodes loci start / end one GRanges row.  map(height = score) makes arch’s peak tall score; peak heights interpreted track’s yscale.
code: arc_gr <- GRanges(   "chr1",   IRanges(start = c(1.2e6, 1.6e6, 2.1e6),           end   = c(1.5e6, 2.0e6, 2.7e6)),   score = c(0.3, 0.7, 0.5) )  seq_plot() %|%   seq_track(track_id = "A",             data = arc_gr,             mapping = map(x = start, y = score),             windows = win) %+%   seq_arc(map(x0 = start, x1 = end,               y0 = score, height = score),           aesthetics = aes(color = "#4385BE", linewidth = 1.2)) -> p p$plot()

title: SeqPlotR Links: Arches, SV Reconstruction, and Cross-Track Connectors
text: aes(curve = ...) controls high arch bulges: "length" (default) — bulge scales genomic span link. "equal" — fixed bulge 0.2 data-units regardless span. numeric value — used directly bulge offset. aes(orientation = "+") arches (default), "-" arches .
code: seq_plot() %|%   seq_track(track_id = "A",             data = arc_gr,             mapping = map(x = start, y = score),             windows = win) %+%   seq_arc(map(x0 = start, x1 = end, height = score),           aesthetics = aes(color = "#AF3029",                            linewidth = 1.5,                            curve = "equal")) -> p p$plot()

what: seq_arch — arch with stems and stubs
title: SeqPlotR Links: Arches, SV Reconstruction, and Cross-Track Connectors
text: seq_arch adds vertical stems anchor’s baseline (y0 / y1) arch endpoints. one anchor falls outside every visible window, seq_arch instead draws half-height stub visible end small angled hook label naming partner chromosome.

previous_headings: seq_arch — arch with stems and stubs
title: SeqPlotR Links: Arches, SV Reconstruction, and Cross-Track Connectors
code: sv_df <- data.frame(   chr1    = "chr1",   start1  = c(1.15e6, 1.40e6, 1.85e6, 2.20e6),   chr2    = "chr1",   start2  = c(1.55e6, 2.05e6, 2.45e6, 2.75e6),   height  = c(0.8,    0.5,    0.7,    0.6),   stringsAsFactors = FALSE )  seq_plot() %|%   seq_track(track_id = "A", windows = win) %+%   seq_arch(data = sv_df,            mapping = map(x0 = start1, x1 = start2,                          chrom0 = chr1, chrom1 = chr2,                          height = height),            aesthetics = aes(color = "#205EA6", linewidth = 1.2)) -> p p$plot()

previous_headings: seq_arch — arch with stems and stubs
title: SeqPlotR Links: Arches, SV Reconstruction, and Cross-Track Connectors
text: anchor lies outside visible window, seq_arch draws stub labelled partner chromosome. Toggle aes(plotStubs = TRUE/FALSE); control hook geometry aes(stubAngle = 45, stubLength = 0.02).
code: # Two in-window pairs and three half-visible (anchor 1 off the right edge, # or onto chr2). stub_df <- data.frame(   chr1 = "chr1",   start1 = c(1.15e6, 1.40e6, 1.85e6, 2.20e6, 2.60e6),   chr2 = c("chr1", "chr1", "chr1", "chr2", "chr1"),   start2 = c(1.55e6, 2.05e6, 4.50e6, 5.00e5, 4.20e6),   height = c(0.7, 0.5, 0.8, 0.6, 0.9),   stringsAsFactors = FALSE )  seq_plot() %|%   seq_track(track_id = "A", windows = win) %+%   seq_arch(data = stub_df,            mapping = map(x0 = start1, x1 = start2,                          chrom0 = chr1, chrom1 = chr2,                          height = height),            aesthetics = aes(color   = "#66800B",                             linewidth = 1.1,                             stubLength = 0.04)) -> p p$plot()

title: SeqPlotR Links: Arches, SV Reconstruction, and Cross-Track Connectors
text: handful synthetic structural variants spanning five classes: Plot canonical recon mapping:  three horizontal guide lines mark inversion / Dup-Del / translocation tiers; HH/TT, DEL/DUP, TRA labels sit left margin.
code: sv_tbl <- data.frame(   chr1    = "chr1",   start1  = c(1.15e6, 1.30e6, 1.55e6, 1.80e6, 2.10e6,               2.35e6, 2.55e6, 2.75e6),   chr2    = c("chr1","chr1","chr1","chr1","chr1",               "chr1","chr1","chr2"),   start2  = c(1.50e6, 1.85e6, 2.05e6, 2.30e6, 2.60e6,               2.85e6, 4.20e6, 5.00e5),   strand1 = c("+","-","-","+","+",               "-","+","+"),   strand2 = c("+","-","+","-","+",               "-","+","+"),   stringsAsFactors = FALSE ) sv_tbl #>   chr1  start1 chr2  start2 strand1 strand2 #> 1 chr1 1150000 chr1 1500000       +       + #> 2 chr1 1300000 chr1 1850000       -       - #> 3 chr1 1550000 chr1 2050000       -       + #> 4 chr1 1800000 chr1 2300000       +       - #> 5 chr1 2100000 chr1 2600000       +       + #> 6 chr1 2350000 chr1 2850000       -       - #> 7 chr1 2550000 chr1 4200000       +       + #> 8 chr1 2750000 chr2  500000       +       + seq_plot() %|%   seq_track(track_id = "SV", windows = win) %+%   seq_recon(data = sv_tbl,             mapping = map(x0 = start1, x1 = start2,                           chrom0 = chr1, chrom1 = chr2,                           strand0 = strand1, strand1 = strand2)) -> p p$plot()

title: SeqPlotR Links: Arches, SV Reconstruction, and Cross-Track Connectors
text: Links compose everything else. example stacks coverage ribbon, structural-variant seq_recon row, gene model track.
code: xs        <- seq(1.05e6, 2.95e6, length.out = 80) mu        <- sin((xs - 1e6) / 3e5) * 0.3 + 0.5 cov_gr    <- GRanges("chr1", IRanges(start = xs, width = 1),                      mean = mu, lo = mu - 0.12, hi = mu + 0.12)  gene_gr <- GRanges(   "chr1",   IRanges(start = c(1.10e6, 1.30e6, 1.55e6, 1.78e6,                     2.05e6, 2.30e6, 2.55e6, 2.78e6),           width = c(1.5e5,  1.8e5,  1.5e5,  2.0e5,                     1.8e5,  1.5e5,  1.8e5,  1.5e5)),   gene_id   = c("A","A","A","A","B","B","B","B"),   gene_name = c("GENEA","GENEA","GENEA","GENEA",                 "GENEB","GENEB","GENEB","GENEB"),   strand_col = c(rep("+", 4), rep("-", 4)),   feature = rep(c("UTR", "exon", "exon", "UTR"), 2),   color   = c(rep("#205EA6", 4), rep("#AF3029", 4)) )  seq_plot() %|%   seq_track(track_id = "Coverage",             data = cov_gr,             mapping = map(x = start, y_min = lo, y_max = hi),             windows = win,             track_height = 1.5) %+%   seq_ribbon(aesthetics = aes(fill = "#4385BE", alpha = 0.5)) %__%   seq_track(track_id = "SV",             windows = win,             track_height = 1.6) %+%   seq_recon(data = sv_tbl,             mapping = map(x0 = start1, x1 = start2,                           chrom0 = chr1, chrom1 = chr2,                           strand0 = strand1, strand1 = strand2)) %__%   seq_track(track_id = "Genes",             data = gene_gr,             mapping = map(group  = gene_id,                           type   = feature,                           strand = strand_col,                           label  = gene_name,                           color  = color),             windows = win,             track_height = 1) %+%   seq_gene(map(group  = gene_id,                type   = feature,                strand = strand_col,                label  = gene_name,                color  = color)) -> p p$plot()

title: SeqPlotR Links: Arches, SV Reconstruction, and Cross-Track Connectors
text: Cross-track links added plot level — referenced tracks defined — t0 / t1 naming two tracks explicitly. Anchors still encoded single data object via BEDPE-style map() vocabulary. recurring subtlety: cross-track link carries data, keep track-level mapping minimal. Anything track’s mapping merged link’s mapping evaluation, expressions like map(x = start, y = logR) track try find logR link’s BEDPE table error . idiomatic fix attach per-track data mapping elements use , track .

what: seq_string — smooth curves between two tracks
title: SeqPlotR Links: Arches, SV Reconstruction, and Cross-Track Connectors
text: seq_string draws cubic Bezier curve anchors t0 t1. strand0 / strand1 fields drive automatic curve-shape inference: matching strands (+/+, -/-) → C curve opposing strands (+/-, -/+) → S curve Force specific shape aes(type = "c") aes(type = "s").

previous_headings: Cross-track connectors > seq_string — smooth curves between two tracks
what: SV breakpoints across copy-number and coverage tracks
title: SeqPlotR Links: Arches, SV Reconstruction, and Cross-Track Connectors
text: simulated copy-number scatter coverage ribbon, three SV breakpoints connecting loci across two tracks. Per-link y0 / y1 values anchor string specific data-scale y track.  string lands (x0, y0) CN track (x1, y1) COV track. Strands drive curve shape: first third links (mixed strands) bend S-curves, middle link (matching strands swap) bends C-curve.
code: xs     <- seq(1.05e6, 2.95e6, length.out = 120) cn_gr  <- GRanges("chr1", IRanges(xs, width = 1),                   logR = sin((xs - 1e6) / 5e5) + rnorm(length(xs), 0, 0.08)) cov_gr <- GRanges("chr1", IRanges(xs, width = 1),                   score = 0.5 + 0.3 * cos((xs - 1e6) / 4e5))  sv_df <- data.frame(   c0 = "chr1", p0 = c(1.30e6, 1.75e6, 2.25e6),   c1 = "chr1", p1 = c(1.60e6, 2.10e6, 2.65e6),   s0 = c("+", "-", "+"),   s1 = c("-", "+", "-"),   y0 = c( 0.8, -0.6,  0.4),   y1 = c( 0.6,  0.7,  0.5),   stringsAsFactors = FALSE )  seq_plot() %|%   seq_track(track_id = "CN",  windows = win, track_height = 1.2) %+%     seq_point(data = cn_gr, mapping = map(x = start, y = logR),               aesthetics = aes(color = "#205EA6", size = 0.35)) %__%   seq_track(track_id = "COV", windows = win, track_height = 1.2) %+%     seq_area(data = cov_gr, mapping = map(x = start, y = score),              aesthetics = aes(fill = "#66800B", alpha = 0.5)) %+%   seq_string(data = sv_df,              map(x0 = p0, x1 = p1, chrom0 = c0, chrom1 = c1,                  strand0 = s0, strand1 = s1,                  y0 = y0, y1 = y1),              t0 = "CN", t1 = "COV",              aesthetics = aes(color = "#AF3029", linewidth = 1.3,                               alpha = 0.75)) -> p p$plot()

title: SeqPlotR Links: Arches, SV Reconstruction, and Cross-Track Connectors
text: Drop strand fields (set aes(type = "c")) render every string C-curve regardless strand:
code: seq_plot() %|%   seq_track(track_id = "CN",  windows = win, track_height = 1.2) %+%     seq_point(data = cn_gr, mapping = map(x = start, y = logR),               aesthetics = aes(color = "#205EA6", size = 0.35)) %__%   seq_track(track_id = "COV", windows = win, track_height = 1.2) %+%     seq_area(data = cov_gr, mapping = map(x = start, y = score),              aesthetics = aes(fill = "#66800B", alpha = 0.5)) %+%   seq_string(data = sv_df,              map(x0 = p0, x1 = p1, chrom0 = c0, chrom1 = c1,                  y0 = y0, y1 = y1),              t0 = "CN", t1 = "COV",              aesthetics = aes(color = "#BC5215", linewidth = 1.3,                               alpha = 0.75, type = "c")) -> p p$plot()

title: SeqPlotR Links: Arches, SV Reconstruction, and Cross-Track Connectors
text: seq_synteny draws filled quadrilateral connecting region t0 homologous region t1. edges region come map(): x0, x0_end — left/right genomic edge track t0 x1, x1_end — left/right genomic edge track t1 y0 / y1 absent map(), polygon attaches bottom edge t0’s inner panel top edge t1’s inner panel — fills gap two tracks exactly.

previous_headings: Cross-track connectors > seq_synteny — filled trapezoids between tracks
title: SeqPlotR Links: Arches, SV Reconstruction, and Cross-Track Connectors
text: Two gene-model tracks representing homologous segments two assemblies, trapezoid per block coloured mapped fill column:  trapezoids start AsmA’s bottom inner edge end AsmB’s top inner edge, left / right corners driven x0 / x0_end x1 / x1_end fields. per-link fill column picked mapping; aes(alpha = ...) applies globally fill.
code: genes_top <- GRanges("chr1",   IRanges(start = c(1.20e6, 1.70e6, 2.30e6),           width = c(2.0e5,  1.5e5,  2.5e5)),   gene_id    = c("G1", "G2", "G3"),   feature    = "exon",   strand_col = c("+", "-", "+"),   color      = c("#205EA6", "#AF3029", "#66800B") ) genes_bot <- GRanges("chr1",   IRanges(start = c(1.25e6, 1.60e6, 2.40e6),           width = c(1.8e5,  2.0e5,  2.2e5)),   gene_id    = c("G1p", "G2p", "G3p"),   feature    = "exon",   strand_col = c("+", "-", "+"),   color      = c("#205EA6", "#AF3029", "#66800B") )  syn_df <- data.frame(   c0 = "chr1",   p0 = c(1.20e6, 1.70e6, 2.30e6),   p0e = c(1.40e6, 1.85e6, 2.55e6),   c1 = "chr1",   p1 = c(1.25e6, 1.60e6, 2.40e6),   p1e = c(1.43e6, 1.80e6, 2.62e6),   col = c("#205EA6", "#AF3029", "#66800B"),   stringsAsFactors = FALSE )  seq_plot() %|%   seq_track(track_id = "AsmA", windows = win, track_height = 0.9) %+%     seq_gene(data = genes_top,              mapping = map(group = gene_id, type = feature,                            strand = strand_col, color = color)) %__%   seq_track(track_id = "AsmB", windows = win, track_height = 0.9) %+%     seq_gene(data = genes_bot,              mapping = map(group = gene_id, type = feature,                            strand = strand_col, color = color)) %+%   seq_synteny(data = syn_df,               map(x0 = p0, x0_end = p0e, x1 = p1, x1_end = p1e,                   chrom0 = c0, chrom1 = c1, fill = col),               t0 = "AsmA", t1 = "AsmB",               aesthetics = aes(alpha = 0.35, linewidth = 0.3)) -> p p$plot()

title: SeqPlotR Links: Arches, SV Reconstruction, and Cross-Track Connectors
text: seq_zoom connects genomic region appears two tracks span different window ranges — chromosome-scale overview stacked zoomed detail panel, example. x0 / x0_end required; x1 / x1_end default region (common case: “show region tracks”). polygon auto-detects track attaches bottom edge; lower track gets top edge. small stem offset (aes(stemOffset = 0.01)) nudges polygon clear track borders.

what: Chromosome-scale overview into a zoomed detail
title: SeqPlotR Links: Arches, SV Reconstruction, and Cross-Track Connectors
text: grey quadrilateral fans narrow slice overview track full width detail panel. Multiple zoom polygons fine — drop rows zoom_region connect one region .
code: # Overview spans 5 Mb; detail zooms into 1.6 – 2.4 Mb. overview_win <- GRanges("chr1", IRanges(1,      5e6)) detail_win   <- GRanges("chr1", IRanges(1.6e6,  2.4e6))  xs_o  <- seq(1,      5e6,   length.out = 160) xs_d  <- seq(1.6e6,  2.4e6, length.out = 120) sig_o <- GRanges("chr1", IRanges(xs_o, width = 1),                  score = 0.5 + 0.3 * sin((xs_o - 1) / 6e5)) sig_d <- GRanges("chr1", IRanges(xs_d, width = 1),                  score = 0.5 + 0.3 * sin((xs_d - 1) / 6e5) +                          rnorm(length(xs_d), 0, 0.03))  zoom_region <- GRanges("chr1", IRanges(1.6e6, 2.4e6))  seq_plot() %|%   seq_track(track_id = "Overview", windows = overview_win,             track_height = 0.5) %+%     seq_area(data = sig_o, mapping = map(x = start, y = score),              aesthetics = aes(fill = "#4385BE", alpha = 0.55)) %__%   seq_track(track_id = "Detail", windows = detail_win,             track_height = 1.3) %+%     seq_area(data = sig_d, mapping = map(x = start, y = score),              aesthetics = aes(fill = "#205EA6", alpha = 0.8)) %+%   seq_zoom(data = zoom_region,            map(x0 = start, x0_end = end),            t0 = "Overview", t1 = "Detail",            aesthetics = aes(fill = "#878580", alpha = 0.2,                             color = "#878580", linewidth = 0.4)) -> p p$plot()

what: Projecting different regions onto the two tracks
title: SeqPlotR Links: Arches, SV Reconstruction, and Cross-Track Connectors
text: Supply separate x1 / x1_end fields region t1 isn’t identical one t0 — useful connecting paralogous translocated segments:
code: zoom_df <- data.frame(   c0   = "chr1", p0  = 1.60e6, p0e  = 2.00e6,   c1   = "chr1", p1  = 1.80e6, p1e  = 2.30e6,   stringsAsFactors = FALSE )  seq_plot() %|%   seq_track(track_id = "Overview", windows = overview_win,             track_height = 0.5) %+%     seq_area(data = sig_o, mapping = map(x = start, y = score),              aesthetics = aes(fill = "#4385BE", alpha = 0.55)) %__%   seq_track(track_id = "Detail", windows = detail_win,             track_height = 1.3) %+%     seq_area(data = sig_d, mapping = map(x = start, y = score),              aesthetics = aes(fill = "#205EA6", alpha = 0.8)) %+%   seq_zoom(data = zoom_df,            map(x0 = p0, x0_end = p0e, x1 = p1, x1_end = p1e,                chrom0 = c0, chrom1 = c1),            t0 = "Overview", t1 = "Detail",            aesthetics = aes(fill = "#AF3029", alpha = 0.18,                             color = "#AF3029", linewidth = 0.4)) -> p p$plot()

what: Fanning out to two detail panels with a patchwork layout
title: SeqPlotR Links: Arches, SV Reconstruction, and Cross-Track Connectors
text: Zoom polygons compose naturally seq_plot(layout = "..."). Drop wide overview top row pair two independent detail panels , emit one seq_zoom per detail region. plot-level links validate t0 t1 refer tracks already added plot, zooms go end chain.   zoom polygon inherits color destination track, left right fan-outs visually distinguishable glance. idiom scales panels — add letters layout string’s second row matching seq_zoom per region.
code: fan_layout <- " AAAA BBCC " seq_preview_layout(layout = fan_layout) # Overview spans chr1:1-5 Mb; B zooms into 1.2-1.8 Mb, C into 3.2-4.0 Mb. overview_win <- GRanges("chr1", IRanges(1,      5e6)) detail_b_win <- GRanges("chr1", IRanges(1.2e6,  1.8e6)) detail_c_win <- GRanges("chr1", IRanges(3.2e6,  4.0e6))  xs_a  <- seq(1,      5e6,   length.out = 200) xs_b  <- seq(1.2e6,  1.8e6, length.out =  80) xs_c  <- seq(3.2e6,  4.0e6, length.out =  80) mk_sig <- function(xs, jitter = 0) {   GRanges("chr1", IRanges(xs, width = 1),           score = 0.5 + 0.3 * sin((xs - 1) / 5e5) +                   rnorm(length(xs), 0, jitter)) } sig_a <- mk_sig(xs_a, jitter = 0.00) sig_b <- mk_sig(xs_b, jitter = 0.03) sig_c <- mk_sig(xs_c, jitter = 0.03)  zoom_b <- GRanges("chr1", IRanges(1.2e6, 1.8e6)) zoom_c <- GRanges("chr1", IRanges(3.2e6, 4.0e6))  seq_plot(layout = fan_layout) %+%   seq_track(track_id = "A", windows = overview_win) %+%     seq_area(data = sig_a, mapping = map(x = start, y = score),              aesthetics = aes(fill = "#4385BE", alpha = 0.55)) %+%   seq_track(track_id = "B", windows = detail_b_win) %+%     seq_area(data = sig_b, mapping = map(x = start, y = score),              aesthetics = aes(fill = "#66800B", alpha = 0.8)) %+%   seq_track(track_id = "C", windows = detail_c_win) %+%     seq_area(data = sig_c, mapping = map(x = start, y = score),              aesthetics = aes(fill = "#AF3029", alpha = 0.8)) %+%   seq_zoom(data = zoom_b, map(x0 = start, x0_end = end),            t0 = "A", t1 = "B",            aesthetics = aes(fill = "#66800B", alpha = 0.22,                             color = "#66800B", linewidth = 0.5)) %+%   seq_zoom(data = zoom_c, map(x0 = start, x0_end = end),            t0 = "A", t1 = "C",            aesthetics = aes(fill = "#AF3029", alpha = 0.22,                             color = "#AF3029", linewidth = 0.5)) -> p p$plot()

title: SeqPlotR Links: Arches, SV Reconstruction, and Cross-Track Connectors
text: seq_highlight draws continuous filled band passes every track t0 t1 (inclusive) genomic coordinates; widths NPC follow track’s scale, band naturally fans compresses across tracks different windows. Unlike seq_zoom (polygon pair tracks), seq_highlight single shape crosses many panels, intended highlighting one loci interest across stack — typical ChIP-seq / ATAC-seq use case.

previous_headings: Cross-track connectors > seq_highlight — multi-track region annotation
title: SeqPlotR Links: Arches, SV Reconstruction, and Cross-Track Connectors
text: highlight region produces single band spans three tracks. Per-track rectangles share inner panel y-range, trapezoids gaps tracks bridge pair.
code: win <- GRanges("chr1", IRanges(1, 1e6)) xs  <- seq(1, 1e6, length.out = 400) mk_sig <- function(seed, freq, jitter = 0.03) {   set.seed(seed)   GRanges("chr1", IRanges(xs, width = 1),           score = 0.5 + 0.3 * sin((xs - 1) / freq) +                   rnorm(length(xs), 0, jitter)) } sig_a <- mk_sig(1, 1.5e5) sig_b <- mk_sig(2, 2.0e5) sig_c <- mk_sig(3, 2.5e5)  # Two genomic regions to flag across all three tracks. hl <- GRanges("chr1", IRanges(c(2.0e5, 6.5e5),                               c(2.6e5, 7.4e5)))  seq_plot() %|%   seq_track(track_id = "A", windows = win) %+%     seq_area(data = sig_a, mapping = map(x = start, y = score),              aesthetics = aes(fill = "#4385BE", alpha = 0.7)) %__%   seq_track(track_id = "B", windows = win) %+%     seq_area(data = sig_b, mapping = map(x = start, y = score),              aesthetics = aes(fill = "#205EA6", alpha = 0.7)) %__%   seq_track(track_id = "C", windows = win) %+%     seq_area(data = sig_c, mapping = map(x = start, y = score),              aesthetics = aes(fill = "#24837B", alpha = 0.7)) %+%   seq_highlight(data = hl, map(x0 = start, x0_end = end),                 t0 = "A", t1 = "C",                 aesthetics = aes(fill = "#AF3029", alpha = 0.18)) -> p p$plot()

previous_headings: Cross-track connectors > seq_highlight — multi-track region annotation
what: Different scales: bands fan and compress
title: SeqPlotR Links: Arches, SV Reconstruction, and Cross-Track Connectors
text: tracks span different genomic ranges, highlight follows track’s scale, producing fanning shape across scale change. Pair overview zoomed detail, drop single seq_highlight, band traces zoom relationship visually:  band narrow inside overview (5 Mb window) wide inside zoomed detail (800 kb window covering region), trapezoid bridging two.
code: overview_win <- GRanges("chr1", IRanges(1,      5e6)) detail_win   <- GRanges("chr1", IRanges(1.6e6,  2.4e6))  xs_o  <- seq(1,      5e6,   length.out = 200) xs_d  <- seq(1.6e6,  2.4e6, length.out = 120) sig_o <- GRanges("chr1", IRanges(xs_o, width = 1),                  score = 0.5 + 0.3 * sin((xs_o - 1) / 6e5)) sig_d <- GRanges("chr1", IRanges(xs_d, width = 1),                  score = 0.5 + 0.3 * sin((xs_d - 1) / 6e5))  # A single 1.8 - 2.2 Mb region of interest. hl <- GRanges("chr1", IRanges(1.8e6, 2.2e6))  seq_plot() %|%   seq_track(track_id = "Overview", windows = overview_win,             track_height = 0.5) %+%     seq_area(data = sig_o, mapping = map(x = start, y = score),              aesthetics = aes(fill = "#4385BE", alpha = 0.55)) %__%   seq_track(track_id = "Detail", windows = detail_win,             track_height = 1.3) %+%     seq_area(data = sig_d, mapping = map(x = start, y = score),              aesthetics = aes(fill = "#205EA6", alpha = 0.8)) %+%   seq_highlight(data = hl, map(x0 = start, x0_end = end),                 t0 = "Overview", t1 = "Detail",                 aesthetics = aes(fill = "#878580", alpha = 0.22,                                  color = "#878580", linewidth = 0.4)) -> p p$plot()

what: Flipped tracks: genomic y with scalar x
title: SeqPlotR Links: Arches, SV Reconstruction, and Cross-Track Connectors
text: Elements links axis-agnostic — transform resolved data coordinates canvas npc without caring axis genomic. track’s orientation comes entirely map(). Write map(y = start / end / mid / width) SeqPlotR auto-flips track: y becomes genomic axis (covering windows extent) x becomes scalar data axis whose range inferred element data. scale_x / scale_y arguments required.  gene sits true genomic coordinate along y-axis; horizontal segment plus terminal point show scalar per-gene statistic. Override auto-inferred scales explicit scale_x = seq_scale_continuous(limits = ...) scale_y = seq_scale_genomic(...) seq_track() need fixed range; operator chain otherwise works either orientation.
code: gene_meta <- GRanges("chr1",   IRanges(start = seq(1.05e6, 2.95e6, length.out = 14),           width = 1e4),   log2fc = rnorm(14, 0, 1.3),   sig    = sample(c("up", "down", "ns"),                   14, replace = TRUE,                   prob = c(0.35, 0.35, 0.3)) ) up_col <- c(up = "#AF3029", down = "#205EA6", ns = "#878580")  # `y = mid` is a genomic special, so SeqPlotR flips the track: the # y-axis becomes genomic (1–3 Mb) and the x-axis carries log2fc. seq_plot() %|%   seq_track(data = gene_meta,             mapping = map(x = log2fc, y = mid, color = sig),             windows = win, track_width = 0.6) %+%   seq_segment(mapping = map(x = 0, x_end = log2fc,                             y = mid, y_end = mid,                             color = sig),               aesthetics = aes(linewidth = 2.2)) %+%   seq_point(aesthetics = aes(size = 0.6)) -> p p$plot()

title: New features: expansion, OOB, titles, margins, inheritance
text: Previously, passing expand = c(0.1, 0) continuous scale visible effect — breaks computed npc transform positions data panel used raw data range. batch, expand honoured end--end: panel carries xplot_range / yplot_range includes expansion, elements map .
code: trk_noexp <- seq_track(   data    = gr, windows = win,   mapping = map(x = xv, y = score),   scale_x = seq_scale_continuous(expand = c(0,   0)),   aesthetics = aes("axis.x.title.label" = "x (no expand)") ) %+% seq_point()  trk_exp <- seq_track(   data    = gr, windows = win,   mapping = map(x = xv, y = score),   scale_x = seq_scale_continuous(expand = c(0.2, 0)),   aesthetics = aes("axis.x.title.label" = "x (20% expansion)") ) %+% seq_point()  (seq_plot() %+% trk_noexp %+% trk_exp)$plot()

title: New features: expansion, OOB, titles, margins, inheritance
text: new oob argument controls happens data falls outside (expanded) axis range. "exclude" (default) drops rows reports count via message(); "perimeter" clamps limit reports.
code: # A subset whose x runs from -3 to 13 — three points sit outside [0, 10]. gr_off <- gr mcols(gr_off)$xv <- jitter(c(-3, mcols(gr)$xv[2:(n-1)], 13), amount = 1)  trk_excl <- seq_track(   data    = gr_off, windows = win,   mapping = map(x = xv, y = score),   scale_x = seq_scale_continuous(limits = c(0, 10), oob = "exclude"),   aesthetics = aes("axis.x.title.label" = "oob = exclude") ) %+% seq_point()  trk_per <- seq_track(   data    = gr_off, windows = win,   mapping = map(x = xv, y = score),   scale_x = seq_scale_continuous(limits = c(0, 10), oob = "perimeter"),   aesthetics = aes("axis.x.title.label" = "oob = perimeter") ) %+% seq_point()  (seq_plot() %+% trk_excl %+% trk_per)$plot() #> 4 out-of-bounds data points excluded! (seq_point) #> 4 out-of-bounds data points plotted! (seq_point)

title: New features: expansion, OOB, titles, margins, inheritance
text: Setting structural axis sub-keys (line, title, ticks, labels, text, gridline) NA blanks piece — contract ggplot’s element_blank(). Sides x / y (default ), x1 / x2 / y1 / y2 (specific axis), top-level axis.* (every axis) work.
code: trk_full <- seq_track(   data = gr, windows = win,   mapping = map(x = xv, y = score) ) %+% seq_point()  trk_no_line_no_ticks <- seq_track(   data = gr, windows = win,   mapping = map(x = xv, y = score),   aesthetics = aes(     "axis.x.line"  = NA,     "axis.x.ticks" = NA,     "axis.x.text"  = NA   ) ) %+% seq_point()  (seq_plot() %+% trk_full %+% trk_no_line_no_ticks)$plot()

title: New features: expansion, OOB, titles, margins, inheritance
text: default axis title now derives first element’s mapping (falling back track’s mapping, seqname scale genomic). can override plain string label styled aes(label = ..., col = ..., size = ...).  Genomic axes (seq_scale_genomic(), auto-built genomic scale user supplies scale_x) get per-window seqname default title:
code: # Element mapping wins over track mapping for the title. trk_a <- seq_track(data = gr, windows = win) %+%          seq_point(mapping = map(x = xv, y = score))  # Manual override via the convenience .label sub-key. trk_b <- seq_track(data = gr, windows = win,                    mapping    = map(x = xv, y = score),                    aesthetics = aes("axis.x.title.label" = "Custom title")) %+%          seq_point()  # Or a full styled aes — sets label and any nested style keys at once. trk_c <- seq_track(   data       = gr, windows = win,   mapping    = map(x = xv, y = score),   aesthetics = aes(axis.x.title = aes(label = "Styled title",                                        col   = "#A0282C",                                        size  = 1.0)) ) %+% seq_point()  (seq_plot() %+% trk_a %+% trk_b %+% trk_c)$plot() multi_win <- GRanges(   c("chr1", "chr2", "chr3"),   IRanges(start = c(1, 1, 1), end = c(1e6, 1e6, 1e6)) ) multi_data <- GRanges(   rep(c("chr1", "chr2", "chr3"), each = 50),   IRanges(start = c(sample(1:1e6, 50),                     sample(1:1e6, 50),                     sample(1:1e6, 50)), width = 1) ) mcols(multi_data)$score <- runif(150, 0, 5)  trk_multi <- seq_track(   data    = multi_data, windows = multi_win,   mapping = map(y = score) ) %+% seq_point()  (seq_plot() %+% trk_multi)$plot()

title: New features: expansion, OOB, titles, margins, inheritance
text: position scales now accept pretty argument: named list additional arguments forwarded base pretty().
code: trk_default <- seq_track(   data = gr, windows = win,   mapping    = map(x = xv, y = score),   scale_x    = seq_scale_continuous(limits = c(0, 10)),   aesthetics = aes("axis.x.title.label" = "default n_breaks = 5") ) %+% seq_point()  trk_pretty <- seq_track(   data = gr, windows = win,   mapping    = map(x = xv, y = score),   scale_x    = seq_scale_continuous(                   limits = c(0, 10),                   pretty = list(n = 10, min.n = 5)),   aesthetics = aes("axis.x.title.label" = "pretty = list(n = 10, min.n = 5)") ) %+% seq_point()  (seq_plot() %+% trk_default %+% trk_pretty)$plot()

title: New features: expansion, OOB, titles, margins, inheritance
text: seq_plot() accepts windows argument track without windows inherits. Genomic scales constructed without explicit windows inherit enclosing track. trims boilerplate many tracks share windows.  Genomic scales also pick enclosing track’s windows constructed bare:
code: # Old style — every track repeats the windows. old <- seq_plot() %+%   (seq_track(data = gr, windows = win,              mapping = map(x = xv, y = score)) %+% seq_point()) %+%   (seq_track(data = gr, windows = win,              mapping = map(x = xv, y = score)) %+% seq_point())  # New style — windows live on the plot. new <- seq_plot(windows = win) %+%   (seq_track(data = gr,              mapping = map(x = xv, y = score)) %+% seq_point()) %+%   (seq_track(data = gr,              mapping = map(x = xv, y = score)) %+% seq_point())  new$plot() trk_bare_scale <- seq_track(   data    = gr, windows = win,   scale_x = seq_scale_genomic()           # no windows arg ) %+% seq_point()  (seq_plot() %+% trk_bare_scale)$plot()

title: New features: expansion, OOB, titles, margins, inheritance
text: seq_plot(plot_margin = 0.02) sets outermost canvas margin (NPC units). default 0.02. Per-side overrides via aesthetics = aes(margins = list(top = ..., ...)) still win.
code: tight  <- seq_plot(plot_margin = 0,    windows = win) %+%           (seq_track(data = gr,                      mapping = map(x = xv, y = score)) %+% seq_point()) roomy  <- seq_plot(plot_margin = 0.08, windows = win) %+%           (seq_track(data = gr,                      mapping = map(x = xv, y = score)) %+% seq_point())  tight$plot() roomy$plot()

title: New features: expansion, OOB, titles, margins, inheritance
text: development aid: setting highlight_margins = TRUE overlays translucent rectangles every margin band. Useful tuning layout chasing stray gap.  Colours: drawn alpha = 0.5.
code: highlighted <- seq_plot(windows = win, highlight_margins = TRUE, plot_margin = 0.05) %+%   (seq_track(data = gr,              mapping = map(x = xv, y = score),              track_outer_margin  = 0.04,              track_inner_margin  = 0.03,              window_outer_margin = 0.02,              window_inner_margin = 0.02) %+%    seq_point()) highlighted$plot()

what: 11. Hi-C fetcher: multi-range and 280 Mb default
title: New features: expansion, OOB, titles, margins, inheritance
text: open_hic()$fetch() now takes single region GRanges may contain multiple ranges; range produces intra-region contact submatrix, results concatenated single BEDPE data frame. previous region1 / region2 interface replaced. max_fetch_bp default raised 5 Mb 280 Mb whole-chromosome fetches work box; check now applies per range rather sum. row returned data frame carries seqname range produced (seqnames1/seqnames2), downstream code can still distinguish per-region contacts.
code: hic <- open_hic("path/to/file.hic", resolution = 25000L)  # Single range — same as before, but uses the new arg name. hic$fetch(GRanges("chr14", IRanges(98e6, 99e6)))  # Multi-range fetch in one call. multi_regions <- GRanges(   c("chr14", "chr15"),   IRanges(start = c(98e6, 50e6), end = c(99e6, 51e6)) ) hic$fetch(multi_regions)

title: New features: expansion, OOB, titles, margins, inheritance

code: showcase <- seq_plot(windows = multi_win, plot_margin = 0.03) %+%   (seq_track(      data    = multi_data,      mapping = map(y = score),      aesthetics = aes(axis.x.title = aes(label = "Position",                                           col   = "#3D4D58"))    ) %+% seq_point()) %+%   (seq_track(      data    = multi_data,      mapping = map(x = start, y = score),      scale_y = seq_scale_continuous(limits = c(0, 4),                                      expand = c(0.05, 0),                                      oob    = "exclude",                                      pretty = list(n = 4))    ) %+% seq_path()) showcase$plot() #> 7 out-of-bounds data points excluded! (seq_path) #> 9 out-of-bounds data points excluded! (seq_path) #> 9 out-of-bounds data points excluded! (seq_path)

title: Genomic Visualization with a Layered Grammar
code: pak::pak("andrewrlynch/SeqPlotR")

title: Genomic Visualization with a Layered Grammar
text: SeqPlotR R6-based genomic visualization package. plots rendered single grid viewport, allows coordinates shared across tracks enables cross-track drawing elements (links, zooms, synteny). rendering pipeline every element follows three stages: initialize() — Accept user arguments, store data, mapping, aesthetics. computation. prep() — Resolve data-driven mappings, clip windows, transform genomic/data coordinates npc canvas coordinates. draw() — Call grid drawing primitives using canvas coordinates produced prep(). three-stage pattern enforced elements must bypassed wrapper functions composite elements.

title: Genomic Visualization with a Layered Grammar
code: SeqPlotR/   DESCRIPTION   NAMESPACE   R/     operators.R        # %+% (single operator, RHS class dispatch); %|% and %__% as convenience aliases; seq_blank()     map_aes.R          # map(), aes(), .resolve_mapping(), .aes_to_gpar()     seq_plot.R         # SeqPlotR6, seq_plot()     seq_track.R        # SeqTrackR6, seq_track()     scale.R            # seq_scale_genomic/continuous/discrete, seq_scale_color_*     element_base.R     # SeqElementR6 base class     link_base.R        # SeqLinkR6 base class     seq_annotation.R   # seq_annotation() — plot-level text/shape overlay     # Primitives     seq_point.R     seq_line.R     seq_segment.R     seq_curve.R     seq_path.R     seq_poly.R     seq_area.R     seq_text.R     # Composites     seq_bar.R     seq_ribbon.R     seq_density.R     seq_tile.R     seq_lollipop.R     seq_gene.R     # Links     seq_arc.R     seq_arch.R     seq_recon.R     seq_string.R     seq_synteny.R     seq_zoom.R     # Wrappers     seq_hic.R     seq_copynumber.R     seq_chip.R     # Utilities     layout.R           # .parse_layout_string(), .build_positional_layout()     coord_ops.R        # .clip_to_windows(), .data_to_npc(), .npc_to_canvas()     preview.R          # seq_preview_layout()     colors.R           # flexoki_palette(), color utilities     utilities.R        # %||%, common helpers   tests/     testthat/       test-operators.R       test-layout.R       test-mapping.R       test-elements.R       test-links.R       test-wrappers.R       test-preview.R   vignettes/     getting-started.Rmd     patchwork-layouts.Rmd     cross-track-links.Rmd     hic-visualization.Rmd   man/                 # auto-generated by Roxygen2

title: Genomic Visualization with a Layered Grammar
text: SeqPlotR uses single %+% operator meaning “include next thing.” Dispatch purely class right-hand side — context field operator-driven state seq_plot object.

title: Genomic Visualization with a Layered Grammar
text: Layout position track relative previous track specified track , operator: direction first track seq_plot() always ignored — implicitly placed top-left. seq_plot(layout = "...") given, direction ignored entirely tracks. layout string fully authoritative; track positions determined solely track_id matching. Tracks track_id present layout string silently skipped.

title: Genomic Visualization with a Layered Grammar
text: Track existence validation SeqLink: %+% receives SeqLink seq_plot, immediately checks t0 t1 resolve track_id already present plot’s track list. either missing, errors clear message naming unresolved track_id. ensures user defines referenced tracks defining link chain. Expression evaluation left--right. seq_plot object mutated -place returned invisibly every step.

title: Genomic Visualization with a Layered Grammar
text: %|% %__% kept shorthand alternatives reduce verbosity common cases: strictly aliases — logic lives %+%. Using %|% %__% non-SeqTrack RHS falls %+% dispatch.
code: `%|%`  <- function(e1, e2) e1 %+% (if (inherits(e2, "SeqTrack")) { e2$direction <- "right"; e2 } else e2) `%__%` <- function(e1, e2) e1 %+% (if (inherits(e2, "SeqTrack")) { e2$direction <- "under"; e2 } else e2) # These are equivalent: seq_plot() %+% seq_track(direction="right") %+% seq_point() %+% seq_track(direction="under") seq_plot() %|% seq_track() %+% seq_point() %__% seq_track() seq_plot() %+%   seq_track(track_id = "Signal", direction = "right", windows = win) %+% seq_area(map(x=start, y=score)) %+%   seq_track(track_id = "CN",     direction = "right", windows = win) %+% seq_point(map(x=start, y=logR))  %+%   seq_track(track_id = "Genes",  direction = "under", windows = win) %+% seq_gene(map(x=start, type=type, strand=strand)) %+%   seq_string(data=links, map(x=start, y=score), data2=links, mapping2=map(x=end, y=score), t0="Signal", t1="CN") %+%   seq_annotation() # Row 1: Signal | CN # Row 2: Genes # Plot-level: string link + annotation (deferred, drawn last)

title: Genomic Visualization with a Layered Grammar
code: layout <- " ##AA ##AA BBBC BBBD " seq_plot(layout = layout) %+%   seq_track(track_id = "A") %+% seq_point() %+%   seq_track(track_id = "B") %+% seq_area()  %+%   seq_track(track_id = "C") %+% seq_bar()   %+%   seq_track(track_id = "D") %+% seq_line()  %+%   seq_annotation() # Positions determined entirely by layout string; direction ignored

title: Genomic Visualization with a Layered Grammar
text: Captures unevaluated R expressions. prep() time, expression evaluated mcols(data) evaluation environment calling frame enclosing environment: Specials (start, end, width, mid) injected directly evaluation environment alongside mcols columns. means bare symbols (map(x = start)) compound expressions (map(x = (start + end) / 2)) resolve correctly single eval() call without branching.
code: map <- function(...) structure(as.list(substitute(list(...)))[-1], class = "SeqMap") .resolve_mapping <- function(data, mapping, env = parent.frame()) {   if (is.null(data) || is.null(mapping)) return(list())   specials <- list(     start = BiocGenerics::start(data),     end   = BiocGenerics::end(data),     width = BiocGenerics::width(data),     mid   = (BiocGenerics::start(data) + BiocGenerics::end(data)) / 2   )   # Inject specials into the eval environment alongside mcols so that both   # bare symbols (map(x = start)) and compound expressions (map(x = (start+end)/2))   # resolve correctly without separate branching.   eval_env <- c(specials, as.list(S4Vectors::mcols(data)))   lapply(mapping, function(expr) eval(expr, envir = eval_env, enclos = env)) }

what: aes() — constant aesthetics and theme keys
title: Genomic Visualization with a Layered Grammar
text: Captures evaluated values. Used two purposes: 1. Element aesthetics — static visual properties: aes(color="blue", linewidth=1.5, alpha=0.8) 2. Theme/axis keys — hierarchical dotted-key namespace controlling track chrome axis appearance. Two equivalent forms accepted: Theme key inheritance: keys follow 3-level hierarchy. missing specific key falls back less-specific parent: - axis.x1.line.col → axis.x.line.col → axis.line.col Hard break legacy names: old flat aesthetic names (xAxisLine, axisColor, trackBackground, etc.) longer accepted. theme control uses dotted-key namespace.
code: aes <- function(...) structure(list(...), class = "SeqAes") # Flat dotted-key form aes(axis.x.line.col = "red", track.background.fill = "white")  # Nested form (flattened automatically) aes(axis.x = aes(line = aes(col = "red")), track = aes(background = aes(fill = "white")))

title: Genomic Visualization with a Layered Grammar
text: seq_scale_continuous(), seq_scale_genomic(), seq_scale_discrete() now accept additional arguments: Internal helpers: .compute_scale_breaks(), .expand_limits(), .compute_minor_breaks(), .merge_scale_with_theme().
code: seq_scale_continuous(   limits       = NULL,   # c(min, max) — explicit range   breaks       = NULL,   # explicit break positions, or a function(limits) -> breaks   minor_breaks = NULL,   # explicit minor break positions, or function, or integer count   expand       = c(0.05, 0),  # c(multiplicative, additive) expansion beyond data range   cap          = "capped",    # "capped", "full", "exact", "ticks"   labels       = NULL    # explicit labels, or a function(breaks) -> labels )

title: Genomic Visualization with a Layered Grammar
text: Elements can assigned specific axis via map(axis.x = 1) map(axis.x = 2) (equivalently axis.y). Default axis 1. enables secondary axes: SeqTrackR6 gains scale_x2, scale_y2, y_windows2, has_axis_x2, has_axis_y2 fields secondary axis support. 4 axes per track (x1/x2/y1/y2).
code: seq_track(windows=win) %+%   seq_line(data=gr1, map(x=start, y=score1)) %+%           # primary y axis   seq_point(data=gr2, map(x=start, y=score2, axis.y=2))    # secondary y axis

title: Genomic Visualization with a Layered Grammar
text: element resolved, following priority applies mapping field independently: Element’s mapping field (specified) Parent seq_track’s mapping field (field element specify) Element’s data (specified) Parent seq_track’s data Inheritance field-level, object-level. element can override y inheriting x data parent track. axis.x/axis.y selector fields follow inheritance rules.

title: Genomic Visualization with a Layered Grammar
code: .aes_to_gpar <- function(a) {   grid::gpar(     col      = a$color %||% a$col,     fill     = a$fill,     lwd      = a$linewidth %||% a$lwd,     lty      = a$linetype,     alpha    = a$alpha,     cex      = a$size,     fontsize = a$fontsize   ) }

title: Genomic Visualization with a Layered Grammar
text: seq_plot maintains internal rows list track lists, built %+% applied: seq_track(direction = "right") appends rows[[current_row]] seq_track(direction = "") pushes new list onto rows appends track first track always starts top-left regardless direction seq_track() margin arguments: Axis title auto-derivation: drawAxes() derives axis titles deparsing mapping$x mapping$y track’s mapping. x-axis title placed centered outer margin band panel; y-axis title placed inner margin band left panel, rotated 90°. Override aes(xAxisTitle="custom label", yAxisTitle="custom label"), suppress aes(xAxisTitle=FALSE, yAxisTitle=FALSE), control font size aes(axisTitleSize=0.8) (default 0.8 cex). layoutGrid() time: row: sum relative track_width values; assign proportional npc x-spans within row’s allocated y-band row: max track_height determines row’s relative height; normalize rows fill canvas minus margins gaps track: GRanges window within track, assign proportional x sub-spans within track’s x-band (using existing window-gap logic THEfunc)

title: Genomic Visualization with a Layered Grammar
text: Parsing: npc coordinate assignment: Margins gaps applied inside region’s bounds layoutGrid() builds panelBounds. # cells: parsed ignored — track, background, border rendered. seq_blank(): -op S3 object. encountered operator chains, occupies patchwork cell ID causes layoutGrid() skip rendering cell entirely.
code: .parse_layout_string <- function(s) {   # 1. Split on newlines, drop blank lines   # 2. Verify all rows have equal character count (error if not)   # 3. Build a nrow x ncol character matrix   # 4. For each unique non-'#' letter:   #    - Find all (row, col) positions   #    - Compute min_row, max_row, min_col, max_col   #    - Verify every cell in that bounding box is the same letter (error if not — non-rectangular regions are invalid)   #    - Store as list: letter -> list(r0, r1, c0, c1)   # 5. Return: list(matrix = m, regions = regions, nrow = nrow, ncol = ncol) } x0 = (c0 - 1) / ncol;  x1 = c1 / ncol y0 = 1 - r1 / nrow;    y1 = 1 - (r0 - 1) / nrow   # y is top-to-bottom in the string

title: Genomic Visualization with a Layered Grammar
text: Detected mapping$y seq_track resolves GRanges special field (start, end, mid, width). detected: track$uses_genomic_y <- TRUE track$y_windows set windows GRanges (separately provided y_windows argument seq_track) layoutGrid() builds y_sub_panels y-axis using proportional sub-panel logic applied x-axis windows Elements within track receive xscale yscale genomic ranges prep()

text: Extends SeqElementR6. Uses BEDPE-style single-data API — anchors encoded one data object (GRanges data.frame), anchor fields distinguished map() naming convention.

title: Genomic Visualization with a Layered Grammar
text: data2, mapping2, resolved2 fields — anchors come data object.
code: t0         = NULL   # track_id string (or integer index fallback) for anchor 0 t1         = NULL   # track_id string (or integer index fallback) for anchor 1 anchor0_gr = NULL   # point GRanges synthesised by resolve() for anchor 0 anchor1_gr = NULL   # point GRanges synthesised by resolve() for anchor 1

title: Genomic Visualization with a Layered Grammar
text: data can either GRanges data.frame. .resolve_mapping() branches accordingly: GRanges: specials injected eval environment include start, end, width, mid, seqnames, strand (latter two GRanges object directly). Metadata columns available name. data.frame: columns available name directly. genomic specials — fields must explicit column references.

title: Genomic Visualization with a Layered Grammar
text: Example GRanges: Example data.frame (BEDPE):
code: # GRanges with paired loci encoded as mcols links_gr <- GRanges("chr1", IRanges(c(100,300), width=1),                     x1=c(400,600), chrom1=c("chr1","chr1"),                     strand0=c("+","-"), strand1=c("+","-"), score=c(0.5,0.8)) seq_arch(data=links_gr, map(x0=start, x1=x1, chrom0=seqnames, chrom1=chrom1,                              strand0=strand0, strand1=strand1, height=score)) bedpe <- data.frame(chrom0="chr1", x0=c(100,300), chrom1="chr1", x1=c(400,600),                     s0=c("+","-"), s1=c("+","-"), score=c(0.5,0.8)) seq_arch(data=bedpe, map(x0=x0, x1=x1, chrom0=chrom0, chrom1=chrom1,                           strand0=s0, strand1=s1, height=score))

title: Genomic Visualization with a Layered Grammar
text: resolving map() fields, SeqLinkR6$resolve() synthesises anchor0_gr anchor1_gr single-width GRanges objects resolved x0/chrom0 x1/chrom1 fields. point GRanges prep() uses findOverlaps() calls. x0 x1 absent resolved fields, resolve() errors immediately clear message. chrom0/chrom1 absent, chromosome input GRanges seqnames used fallback (single-chromosome GRanges data).

title: Genomic Visualization with a Layered Grammar
text: Tracks referenced track_id string (e.g. t0 = "Signal"). Integer index accepted fallback. track_id takes priority.

title: Genomic Visualization with a Layered Grammar
text: Inside seq_track (via %+%): t0 t1 locked parent track’s track_id. override permitted. seq_plot level (via %+%): stored seq_plot$plot_links (deferred). t0 t1 must specified explicitly. Referenced tracks must already exist operator chain.
code: seq_track(track_id = "A", ...) %+% seq_arc(map(x0=start, x1=end)) # t0 = t1 = "A" automatically seq_plot() %|%   seq_track(track_id="A", ...) %+% seq_point() %|%   seq_track(track_id="B", ...) %+% seq_bar() %+%   seq_string(data=links_gr, map(x0=start, x1=end, chrom0=seqnames, chrom1=seqnames),              t0="A", t1="B")

title: Genomic Visualization with a Layered Grammar
text: layout_all_tracks: named list panel bounds, keyed track_id track_windows_list: named list GRanges windows, keyed track_id plot_track_index: track_id parent track (fallback within-track links) Inside prep(), link: 1. Looks t0 t1 layout_all_tracks (track_id, integer index) 2. Finds overlaps self$anchor0_gr t0’s windows 3. Finds overlaps self$anchor1_gr t1’s windows 4. Resolves canvas npc coordinates matched pair using track’s xscale/yscale/inner
code: prep = function(layout_all_tracks, track_windows_list, plot_track_index = NULL)

code: seq_preview_layout <- function(   plot_obj = NULL,         # a seq_plot object (from operator chain)   layout   = NULL,         # OR a raw layout string   labels   = TRUE,         # show track_id labels in each cell   colors   = NULL,         # optional named vector: track_id -> fill color   margins  = TRUE          # show margin zones )

title: Genomic Visualization with a Layered Grammar
text: function opens new graphics device, calls grid.newpage(), renders: One filled rectangle per unique track region, using soft distinct colors (cycling small qualitative palette colors specified) track_id letter label centered rectangle, bold Light grey cells # / blank regions thin outer border around full canvas indicate margin zone margins = TRUE axes, data, windows — purely structural

title: Genomic Visualization with a Layered Grammar
text: Returns parsed layout metadata (region bounding boxes npc coordinates) invisibly, can inspected piped tooling.

title: Genomic Visualization with a Layered Grammar
code: layout <- " ##AA ##AA BBBC BBBD " seq_preview_layout(layout = layout) # Renders: blank top-left, A top-right, B bottom-left, #          C middle-right, D bottom-right

title: Genomic Visualization with a Layered Grammar
text: Builds seq_plot single seq_track configured genomic x y axes. data: sparse GRanges mcols columns (start bin), j (end bin), score; square numeric matrix rownames/colnames bin coordinates Internally uses seq_tile coordinate-transformed polygons rotate = TRUE: square cell transformed diamond/triangle via rotation matrix applied prep() — approach THEfunc’s SeqTile rotation, viewport transform Returns composable seq_plot

title: Genomic Visualization with a Layered Grammar
text: Builds seq_plot one track: - seq_point raw log-ratio scatter - Optionally overlays seq_segment segment means segment_data provided - Auto-detects column names: tries log2ratio, logR, log2R, ratio points; seg.mean, seg_mean, mean segments; falls back first numeric mcols column warning

title: Genomic Visualization with a Layered Grammar
text: Builds seq_plot one track (signal) optionally second track (genes): - seq_area coverage signal, map(x = start, y = score) auto-detected user-overridden via ... - show_genes GRanges, appends second track via %__% seq_gene(data = show_genes)

title: SeqGene R6 class — SeqGeneR6
text: exon_height Proportion per-tier height allocated exon box. label_pad Minimum padding (bp) around gene's extent keep labels overlapping neighbouring genes. label_cex Expansion factor gene label text. label_offset Horizontal offset (npc units) applied placing labels flush gene backbone. backbone_type Character. One "arrow", "solid", "dashed". Controls gene backbone line rendered. Default "arrow". show_start Logical. TRUE, TSS flag arrow drawn first exon (row order) gene. Default FALSE. tss_position Named list length-2 numeric vectors. Keys gene IDs (matching group values); values c(start, end) genomic coordinates overriding auto-detected first-exon TSS position. Default NULL (auto first exon row order). separate_strands Logical. TRUE, genes placed two horizontal sub-bands strand ("+" top, "-" bottom), labelled. Silently ignored strands "*" one strand present. Default FALSE. style_type Character. One "exon", "gene", "point". Selects per-gene rendering style: "exon" draws backbone + exon/UTR boxes (default); "gene" draws single chevron-shaped polygon spanning full gene extent; "point" draws single filled circle TSS. Default "exon".

NULL patchwork mode. layout_str Raw layout string — patchwork mode (authoritative). aesthetics SeqAes plot-wide aesthetics. tracks Flat list SeqTrackR6 — populated patchwork mode. plot_links Plot-level SeqLink objects — deferred, drawn last. plot_annotations Plot-level SeqAnnotation objects — deferred. layout Layout metadata produced $layoutGrid() — list panelBounds trackBounds. flat_theme merged flat theme map (plot-level default theme user overrides). Populated layoutGrid() time. show_legend Logical. Global legend switch. FALSE, legend output produced track. Default TRUE. windows Optional plot-wide GRanges. Inherited track whose windows NULL. Genomic scales without explicit windows also inherit enclosing track. plot_margin Scalar (length-4 c(b, l, t, r)) used outermost canvas margin. Defaults 0.02. highlight_margins Logical. TRUE, draw translucent overlays every margin band — development aid.

text: data GRanges default data elements track. mapping SeqMap default mapping elements track. aesthetics SeqAes default constant aesthetics. windows GRanges windows track (place windows set). track_id Character unique identifier track. track_width Relative width unit within row. track_height Relative height unit within column. direction One "right" "". elements List SeqElement / SeqLink added via %+%. scale_x Optional position scale primary x axis. scale_y Optional position scale primary y axis. scale_x2 Optional position scale secondary x axis. Populated automatically layoutGrid() time elements whose map(axis.x = 2) targets secondary x axis. scale_y2 Optional position scale secondary y axis. uses_genomic_y TRUE mapping$y resolves genomic special. y_windows Optional GRanges windows primary y axis. y_windows2 Optional GRanges windows secondary y axis. has_axis_x2 TRUE element targets axis.x = 2, scale_x2 explicitly set, theme forces visibility. Set layoutGrid() time. has_axis_y2 Symmetric has_axis_x2 y direction. resolved_theme Populated layoutGrid(): nested list per-axis specs (x1/x2/y1/y2), track chrome, per-window flag. track_outer_margin Named list track-level outer margin (npc) — reserves band axis titles sit. track_inner_margin Named list track-level inner margin (npc) — separates axis titles window row. window_outer_margin Named list per-window outer margin (npc). window_inner_margin Named list per-window inner margin (npc) — holds per-window axis ticks labels. window_margin Deprecated. Set aes("window.gap.width" = <value>) plot track instead. Stored backward compatibility effect layout. combine_windows Logical. TRUE, multi-region windows concatenated single virtual panel, per-original- window axis labels thin separator window boundary. Used draw cross-window data (e.g. inter-chromosomal Hi-C contacts) within one continuous track. combine_y_windows Logical. Symmetric combine_windows y-axis (genomic y tracks ). flip_x Logical. TRUE, mirror x-axis: low data values render right edge panel high values left. Tick labels follow orientation. flip_y Logical. Symmetric flip_x y-axis. Hi-C triangle style produces downward-pointing triangle; diagonal shows lower diagonal. show_legend Logical. FALSE, track contributes legend keys regardless legend fields elements. Default TRUE. window_scale Numeric vector NULL. Per-window x-axis scale factors (e.g. 1e-6 Mb, 1e-3 kb, 1 bp). NULL (default), scale inferred narrowest window. length 1, value applied windows. length equals number windows, values applied positionally. length triggers warning recycles rep_len().

Length 1 applies value windows; length equal number windows uses values positionally; length triggers warning recycles rep_len(). ... Additional arguments (currently ignored).

what: Build genome windows from region strings — create_genome_windows
title: Build genome windows from region strings — create_genome_windows
text: Parses vector region strings — either "chr:start-end" ranges bare chromosome names — GRanges whole-chromosome sub-chromosomal windows. Bare chromosome names expand full length chromosome supplied genome (via default_genome_windows() genome = "hg38"). Overlapping / adjacent regions merged via GenomicRanges::reduce() return.

title: Build genome windows from region strings — create_genome_windows
code: create_genome_windows(regions, padding = 0, genome = "hg38", add_chr = TRUE)

title: Build genome windows from region strings — create_genome_windows
text: regions Character vector region strings. element either "chr1:1000-2000" / "1:1,000-2,000" (commas tolerated) bare chromosome name ("chr1", "X", etc.). "chr" prefix optional input controlled output add_chr. padding Integer. Base pairs extend side every region. Clipped start >= 1. Default 0. genome Reference genome bare-chromosome expansion. "hg38" supported. add_chr Logical. TRUE (default), output seqnames carry "chr" prefix.

title: Build genome windows from region strings — create_genome_windows
text: sorted, non-overlapping GRanges windows.

title: ChIP-style multi-track signal + peaks plot — seq_chip
text: data Either named list GRanges (one per sample), single GRanges sample column (pass name via sample_col). signal GRanges must carry numeric signal column (auto-detected). windows GRanges defining view region. sample_col Column data giving sample identity data single GRanges. Ignored list input. signal_col Explicit signal column name; auto-detected per sample NULL. peaks Optional peak calls, mirroring data: either named list GRanges names, single GRanges sample_col. peak_col Optional column peaks used bar height; default renders uniform-height bars. colors Named character vector mapping sample name colour. Defaults cycling flexoki_palette(). scale_max Numeric scalar named vector capping signal y- axis per sample. NULL (default) autoscales. signal_height Relative height signal track. peak_height Relative height peak track. show_genes Optional GRanges gene annotation — adds final seq_gene() track beneath sample tracks. track_id_prefix Prefix prepended auto-generated track_ids. Useful composing multiple seq_chip() calls via seq_resolve(). legend LegendKey SeqLegendSpec forwarded signal area element. NULL (default) produces legend entry. show_legend Logical. FALSE, signal area elements contribute legend. Default TRUE. ... Additional arguments forwarded seq_track().

title: Draw gene models — seq_gene
text: data Optional GRanges. mapping Optional SeqMap. Recognised fields: group (feature--gene grouping; features sharing value one gene), strand (default "+"), label (defaults group value), type ("exon" full-height box, "UTR" 80-percent-height box, anything else box), color (per-feature color). aesthetics Optional SeqAes. Supports color (default "gray30"), linewidth, alpha. backbone_type Character. One "arrow" (default), "solid", "dashed". Controls backbone line style whether chevron arrows drawn. Ignored style_type "gene" "point" (backbone exists modes). show_start Logical. TRUE, TSS flag arrow drawn first exon gene. Default FALSE. Ignored style_type = "point" (point marks TSS). tss_position Named list overriding per-gene TSS genomic positions. Keys gene IDs; values c(start, end) genomic coordinates. Default NULL (auto-detected first exon row order). separate_strands Logical. TRUE, genes split "+" (top) "-" (bottom) sub-bands, labelled. Silently ignored one strand present. Default FALSE. style_type Character. One "exon" (default), "gene", "point". Selects per-gene rendering style: "exon" draws full backbone exon/UTR boxes; "gene" draws single chevron-shaped polygon spanning gene extent (exon detail); "point" draws single filled circle TSS. Labels drawn three modes. ... Additional arguments reserved future use.

title: Hi-C contact matrix — seq_hic
text: Builds seq_plot() rendering Hi-C contact matrix one four styles: "full" Symmetric square heatmap genomic position x- y-axes. "diagonal" coordinate system "full" (kept separate keyword call sites can switch styles without changing shape). "triangle" Rotated 45 degrees; upper triangle , y-axis interaction distance base pairs. "rectangle" rotation "triangle", y-axis capped max_dist, yielding rectangle.

title: Hi-C contact matrix — seq_hic
code: seq_hic(   data,   windows,   style = "triangle",   max_dist = NULL,   palette = "blues",   na_color = "#FFFFD9",   y_windows = NULL,   combine_windows = FALSE,   combine_y_windows = FALSE,   flip_x = FALSE,   flip_y = FALSE,   track_height = 1,   track_id = NULL,   legend = NULL,   show_legend = TRUE,   ... )

title: Hi-C contact matrix — seq_hic
text: data Sparse GRanges (mcols: i_start, i_end, j_start, j_end, score) numeric matrix / data.frame whose row/column names encode bin positions. row must describe well-formed contact i_end - i_start j_end - j_start equal Hi-C bin width (define tile's footprint position distance axes). cross-chromosomal contacts, include optional j_chrom mcols column giving j-bin's chromosome (GRanges's seqnames taken -bin's chromosome). absent, bins assumed live chromosome. windows GRanges defining genomic region(s) display x-axis. Multiple ranges produce side--side panels (one per range), useful comparing several regions. style One "full", "diagonal", "triangle", "rectangle". Default "triangle". max_dist style = "rectangle" : cap distance axis value (bp). Required "rectangle". palette Colour scale palette tile fill. Passed seq_scale_color_continuous(). na_color Colour zero/NA contacts. y_windows Optional GRanges genomic y-axis range "full" / "diagonal" styles. Defaults windows (square matrix). Pass different GRanges display asymmetric region pair. Ignored rotated styles. combine_windows Logical; TRUE, multi-region windows concatenated single virtual track cross-window data (e.g. inter-chromosomal contacts) renders continuously one panel. Default FALSE. combine_y_windows Symmetric combine_windows multi-region y_windows full / diagonal styles. flip_x, flip_y Logical. Mirror x y axis. triangle style flip_y = TRUE produces downward-pointing triangle; diagonal switches lower diagonal; full flips matrix vertically (y) horizontally (x). Tick labels follow orientation. Default FALSE. track_height Relative track height. track_id track_id generated track. Defaults paste0("hic_", style). legend LegendKey SeqLegendSpec forwarded tile element. NULL (default) produces legend entry. show_legend Logical. FALSE, tile element contributes legend. Default TRUE. ... Additional arguments forwarded seq_track().

title: Hi-C contact matrix — seq_hic
